1262: async function main() {
1263:   const started = now();
1264:   const actions = [];
1265:   let lockHeld = false;
1266: 
1267:   try {
1268:     installSignalHandlers();
1269:     // Lease cleanup runs unconditionally regardless of child termination path.
1270:     process.on('exit', clearAllLeaseFiles);
1271:     refreshPaths();
1272:     debug(`DB: ${advisorDbPath()}`);
1273: 
1274:     const strictSingleWriter = !isStrictModeDisabled(process.env.MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER);
1275:     ownerLeaseRequired = strictSingleWriter;
1276:     if (strictSingleWriter) {
1277:       if (await checkStrictSingleWriter()) return;
1278:     } else {
1279:       log('MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER is disabled; skipping lease check');
1280:     }
1281: 
1282:     lockHeld = await acquireBootstrapLock();
1283:     if (lockHeld) {
1284:       if (Number.isInteger(pendingBootstrapReapPid) && pendingBootstrapReapPid > 0) {
1285:         // The bootstrap lock is held through replacement launch, so stale-child cleanup
1286:         // and spawn share one serialization point without a separate respawn lock.
1287:         const reapOwnerLease = readOwnerLeaseFile();
1288:         const reapExecutablePath = reapOwnerLease?.ownerPid === pendingBootstrapReapPid ? reapOwnerLease.executablePath : null;
1289:         const reapResult = await reapOwnerBeforeRespawn(pendingBootstrapReapPid, reapExecutablePath);
1290:         if (!reapResult.allowed) {
1291:           log(`stale launcher lease reap skipped; ${reapResult.reason} for daemonPid=${pendingBootstrapReapPid}`);
1292:           clearOwnerLeaseFile();
1293:           return;
1294:         }
1295:         pendingBootstrapReapPid = null;
1296:       }
1297:       buildIfNeeded(actions);
1298:       writeState({
1299:         command: 'mk-skill-advisor-launcher',
1300:         start: started,
1301:         end: now(),
1302:         status: 'ready',
1303:         actions,
1304:         server: rel(serverEntrypoint()),
1305:         database: rel(advisorDbPath()),
1306:       });
1307: 
1308:       // PID guard write + reprobe stay inside the bootstrap-lock critical section.
1309:       // Closes the visual race window: two
1310:       // launchers cannot simultaneously pass the strict-single-writer check and race to write
1311:       // their PID guards because both writers serialize on the bootstrap lock.
1312:       if (strictSingleWriter && !ownsOwnerLeaseFile(process.pid)) {
1313:         log('bootstrap reprobe lost owner lease before PID guard write');
1314:         return;
1315:       }
1316:       writeLeaseFile();
1317:       const reprobe = readLeaseFile();
1318:       if (!reprobe || reprobe.pid !== process.pid) {
1319:         const startedAt = reprobe?.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
1320:         clearOwnerLeaseFile();
1321:         process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${reprobe ? reprobe.pid : 'unknown'} startedAt=${startedAt}\n`);
1322:         return;
1323:       }
1324:     }
1325: 
1326:     const launched = launchServer();
1327:     if (!launched) return;
1328:     if (isModelServerEnabled()) {
1329:       if (!hfControl) throw new Error('SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_MODEL_SERVER_ENABLED=1 but model-server supervision lib is unavailable');
1330:       void hfControl.startDemandListener().catch((error) => {
1331:         log(error.stack || error.message);
1332:       });
1333:     }
1334:   } catch (error) {
1335:     try {
1336:       writeState({
1337:         command: 'mk-skill-advisor-launcher',
1338:         start: started,
1339:         end: now(),
1340:         status: 'failed',
1341:         actions,
1342:         error: error.message,
1343:       });
1344:     } catch {
1345:       // If state logging itself fails, stderr still carries the actionable error.
1346:     }
1347:     log(`launcher failed: ${error.message}`);
1348:     debug(error.stack || error.message);
1349:     process.exit(1);
1350:   } finally {
1351:     if (lockHeld) {
1352:       fs.rmSync(lockDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
1353:     }
1354:   }
1355: }
1356: 
1357: function configureLauncherPathsForTesting(nextPaths) {
1358:   if (nextPaths.skillsDir) skillsDir = nextPaths.skillsDir;
1359:   if (nextPaths.kitDir) kitDir = nextPaths.kitDir;
1360:   if (nextPaths.mcpDir) mcpDir = nextPaths.mcpDir;
1361:   if (nextPaths.dbDir) dbDir = nextPaths.dbDir;
1362:   if (nextPaths.lockDir) lockDir = nextPaths.lockDir;
1363:   if (nextPaths.stateFile) stateFile = nextPaths.stateFile;
1364:   systemSpecKitDbDir = nextPaths.systemSpecKitDbDir || path.join(skillsDir, 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'database');
1365: }
1366: 
1367: if (require.main === module) {
1368:   void main();
1369: }
1370: 
1371: module.exports = {
1372:   acquireBootstrapLock,
1373:   acquireOwnerLeaseFile,
1374:   artifactsReady,
1375:   bridgeStdioThroughSessionProxy,
1376:   classifyOwnerLease,
1377:   classifySkillAdvisorFrame,
1378:   clearOwnerLeaseFile,
1379:   configureLauncherPathsForTesting,
1380:   createChildEnv,
1381:   latestSourceMtimeMs,
1382:   ownerLeasePath,
1383:   readOwnerLeaseFile,
1384:   readProcessExecutableBasename,
1385:   reapOwnerBeforeRespawn,
1386:   removeStaleBootstrapLock,
1387:   startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat,
1388:   SKILL_ADVISOR_REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES,
1389:   SKILL_ADVISOR_UNSAFE_TOOL_NAMES,
1390: };

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // --------------------------------------------------------------------------
3: // COMPONENT: Code Index CLI Shim
4: // PURPOSE: Runs the built daemon-backed mk-code-index CLI.
5: // --------------------------------------------------------------------------
6: 'use strict';
7: 
8: const fs = require('fs');
9: const path = require('path');
10: const crypto = require('crypto');
11: const { spawnSync } = require('child_process');
12: 
13: const opencodeDir = path.resolve(__dirname, '..');
14: const skillDir = path.join(opencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-code-graph');
15: const cliDist = path.join(skillDir, 'mcp_server', 'dist', 'code-index-cli.js');
16: const sourceHashState = path.join(path.dirname(cliDist), '.code-index-cli-source-hash.json');
17: const defaultSocketDir = '/tmp/mk-code-index';
18: const socketFileName = 'daemon-ipc.sock';
19: const allowStale = process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_INDEX_CLI_DEV_ALLOW_STALE === '1';
20: 
21: function fail(message) {
22:   process.stderr.write(`${message}\n`);
23:   process.exit(69);
24: }
25: 
26: // Recursively collects the CLI's compiled-source surface (entry, local
27: // imports, build tsconfig) so new files under candidate directories are
28: // watched without editing this list. node_modules and dist stay out of scope.
29: function sourceCandidates() {
30:   const candidates = [
31:     path.join(skillDir, 'mcp_server', 'code-index-cli.ts'),
32:     path.join(skillDir, 'mcp_server', 'code-index-cli-manifest.ts'),
33:     path.join(skillDir, 'mcp_server', 'tool-schemas.ts'),
34:     path.join(skillDir, 'tsconfig.json'),
35:   ];
36:   const files = [];
37: 
38:   const visit = (candidate) => {
39:     if (!fs.existsSync(candidate)) return;
40:     const stat = fs.statSync(candidate);
41:     if (stat.isDirectory()) {
42:       for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(candidate)) {
43:         visit(path.join(candidate, entry));
44:       }
45:       return;
46:     }
47:     if (candidate.endsWith('.ts') || candidate.endsWith('.json')) {
48:       files.push(candidate);
49:     }
50:   };
51: 
52:   for (const candidate of candidates) visit(candidate);
53:   return files;
54: }
55: 
56: function hashSourceFiles(existingSources) {
57:   const hash = crypto.createHash('sha256');
58:   for (const filePath of [...existingSources].sort()) {
59:     hash.update(path.relative(skillDir, filePath));
60:     hash.update('\0');
61:     hash.update(fs.readFileSync(filePath));
62:     hash.update('\0');
63:   }
64:   return hash.digest('hex');
65: }
66: 
67: function readStoredSourceHash() {
68:   try {
69:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(sourceHashState, 'utf8'));
70:     return typeof parsed?.sourceHash === 'string' ? parsed.sourceHash : null;
71:   } catch {
72:     return null;
73:   }
74: }
75: 
76: function writeStoredSourceHash(sourceHash) {
77:   try {
78:     fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(sourceHashState), { recursive: true });
79:     fs.writeFileSync(sourceHashState, `${JSON.stringify({ version: 1, sourceHash })}\n`);
80:   } catch {
81:     // Freshness metadata is an optimization; stale detection remains conservative.
82:   }
83: }
84: 
85: function ensureFreshDist() {
86:   if (!fs.existsSync(cliDist)) {
87:     fail(`code-index dist entrypoint is missing: ${cliDist}. Run tsc -p .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/tsconfig.json.`);
88:   }
89:   if (allowStale) return;
90:   const existingSources = sourceCandidates();
91:   if (existingSources.length === 0) return;
92:   const currentSourceHash = hashSourceFiles(existingSources);
93:   if (readStoredSourceHash() === currentSourceHash) return;
94:   const newestSourceMtime = Math.max(...existingSources.map((filePath) => fs.statSync(filePath).mtimeMs));
95:   const distMtime = fs.statSync(cliDist).mtimeMs;
96:   if (newestSourceMtime > distMtime) {
97:     fail('code-index dist entrypoint is stale. Run tsc -p .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/tsconfig.json.');
98:   }
99:   writeStoredSourceHash(currentSourceHash);
100: }
101: 
102: function ensureSocketDir() {
103:   if (!process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR) {
104:     process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR = defaultSocketDir;
105:   }
106:   const socketDir = process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR;
107:   if (socketDir.startsWith('tcp://')) return;
108:   fs.mkdirSync(socketDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
109:   const socketPath = path.join(path.resolve(socketDir), socketFileName);
110:   if (process.platform === 'darwin' && Buffer.byteLength(socketPath) > 103) {
111:     fail(`code-index socket path exceeds the Darwin sun_path limit: ${socketPath}. Set SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR to a shorter directory or a tcp:// endpoint.`);
112:   }
113: }
114: 
115: ensureSocketDir();
116: ensureFreshDist();
117: 
118: const result = spawnSync(process.execPath, [cliDist, ...process.argv.slice(2)], {
119:   cwd: path.dirname(opencodeDir),
120:   env: process.env,
121:   stdio: 'inherit',
122: });
123: 
124: if (result.error) {
125:   process.stderr.write(`${result.error.message}\n`);
126:   process.exit(75);
127: }
128: 
129: process.exit(result.status ?? 1);

(End of file - total 129 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/bin/worktree-session.sh</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: #
3: # worktree-session.sh — launch an AI coding session in its own isolated git worktree.
4: #
5: # A top-level (human-launched) session gets its own worktree + branch + isolated MCP
6: # databases, so concurrent sessions on different runtimes never share a working tree or
7: # contend on the single-writer DB lease. An orchestrated child (subagent / dispatched
8: # task / deep-loop iteration) shares its parent's worktree instead of nesting.
9: #
10: # Usage:
11: #   worktree-session.sh <runtime> [cli-args...]
12: #   worktree-session.sh --dry-run <runtime> [cli-args...]   # print the plan, change nothing
13: #
14: # <runtime> is the CLI to exec (claude, codex, opencode, ...).
15: #
16: # Child detection (either signal => exec in place, no new worktree):
17: #   1. AI_SESSION_CHILD=1 in the environment (set at dispatch sites for orchestrated children).
18: #   2. Structural backstop: the process is already inside a linked git worktree
19: #      (git --git-dir differs from --git-common-dir).
20: # On an unknown/ambiguous signal we default to top-level (isolate) — the safe failure mode.
21: #
22: # DB isolation is hybrid: shared node_modules/dist are symlinked from the main checkout
23: # (no per-worktree reinstall/rebuild), but each worktree gets its own MCP databases via
24: # SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR + SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR.
25: #
26: # Socket-length workaround: the daemon's unix-domain IPC socket defaults to <db-dir>/
27: # daemon-ipc.sock. A worktree DB dir nested deep inside the repo blows past the platform
28: # sun_path limit (~104 bytes on macOS, ~108 on Linux) and the daemon dies with EINVAL on
29: # listen(). So we also point SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR at a short per-session dir under $HOME,
30: # keeping the socket path well under the limit while the DBs stay in the worktree.
31: 
32: set -euo pipefail
33: 
34: DRY_RUN=0
35: if [ "${1:-}" = "--dry-run" ]; then DRY_RUN=1; shift || true; fi
36: 
37: RUNTIME="${1:-}"
38: if [ -z "$RUNTIME" ]; then
39:   echo "usage: worktree-session.sh [--dry-run] <runtime> [cli-args...]" >&2
40:   exit 2
41: fi
42: shift || true
43: 
44: log() { echo "[worktree-session] $*" >&2; }
45: 
46: # Shared artifacts symlinked from main into each worktree (deps + compiled output).
47: # Override with SPECKIT_WORKTREE_SHARED_PATHS (newline- or colon-separated relative paths).
48: default_shared_paths() {
49:   cat <<'PATHS'
50: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/node_modules
51: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/node_modules
52: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist
53: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist
54: .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/node_modules
55: .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist
56: PATHS
57: }
58: 
59: exec_in_place() {
60:   local reason="$1"
61:   log "child/already-isolated session ($reason) — exec'ing in place, no new worktree"
62:   if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "1" ]; then
63:     echo "DRY_RUN: would exec in place: $RUNTIME $*"
64:     exit 0
65:   fi
66:   exec "$RUNTIME" "$@"
67: }
68: 
69: # --- Child detection -------------------------------------------------------
70: if [ "${AI_SESSION_CHILD:-}" = "1" ]; then
71:   exec_in_place "AI_SESSION_CHILD=1" "$@"
72: fi
73: 
74: GIT_DIR="$(git rev-parse --git-dir 2>/dev/null || true)"
75: GIT_COMMON_DIR="$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir 2>/dev/null || true)"
76: if [ -n "$GIT_DIR" ] && [ -n "$GIT_COMMON_DIR" ]; then
77:   # Normalize to absolute for a reliable comparison.
78:   GIT_DIR_ABS="$(cd "$(dirname "$GIT_DIR")" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)/$(basename "$GIT_DIR")" || GIT_DIR_ABS="$GIT_DIR"
79:   GIT_COMMON_ABS="$(cd "$(dirname "$GIT_COMMON_DIR")" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)/$(basename "$GIT_COMMON_DIR")" || GIT_COMMON_ABS="$GIT_COMMON_DIR"
80:   if [ "$GIT_DIR_ABS" != "$GIT_COMMON_ABS" ]; then
81:     exec_in_place "already inside a linked worktree" "$@"
82:   fi
83: fi
84: 
85: # --- Top-level session: allocate an isolated worktree ----------------------
86: MAIN_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || true)"
87: if [ -z "$MAIN_ROOT" ]; then
88:   log "not inside a git repository — exec'ing in place (cannot isolate)"
89:   if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "1" ]; then echo "DRY_RUN: not a git repo; would exec in place"; exit 0; fi
90:   exec "$RUNTIME" "$@"
91: fi
92: 
93: # Short unique slug (epoch + pid). bash has no Date.now restriction.
94: SLUG="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-$$"
95: WT_REL=".worktrees/${RUNTIME}-${SLUG}"
96: WT_ABS="$MAIN_ROOT/$WT_REL"
97: BRANCH="work/${RUNTIME}/${SLUG}"
98: 
99: # Resolve the shared-artifact list.
100: if [ -n "${SPECKIT_WORKTREE_SHARED_PATHS:-}" ]; then
101:   SHARED_RAW="$(printf '%s' "$SPECKIT_WORKTREE_SHARED_PATHS" | tr ':' '\n')"
102: else
103:   SHARED_RAW="$(default_shared_paths)"
104: fi
105: 
106: WT_DB_DIR="$WT_ABS/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database"
107: WT_CG_DB_DIR="$WT_ABS/.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database"
108: # Short per-session socket dir under $HOME (NOT inside the deep worktree) to stay under the
109: # platform sun_path limit. The reaper cleans these alongside merged worktrees.
110: SOCK_DIR="$HOME/.spk-wt-sock/${RUNTIME}-${SLUG}"
111: 
112: if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "1" ]; then
113:   echo "DRY_RUN PLAN"
114:   echo "  main_root      = $MAIN_ROOT"
115:   echo "  worktree       = $WT_ABS"
116:   echo "  branch         = $BRANCH"
117:   echo "  SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR        = $WT_DB_DIR"
118:   echo "  SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR = $WT_CG_DB_DIR"
119:   echo "  SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR = $SOCK_DIR  (socket path len $(( ${#SOCK_DIR} + 16 )), platform limit ~104)"
120:   echo "  symlink shared paths (those present in main):"
121:   while IFS= read -r rel; do
122:     [ -z "$rel" ] && continue
123:     if [ -e "$MAIN_ROOT/$rel" ]; then echo "    + $rel"; else echo "    - $rel (absent in main, skip)"; fi
124:   done <<< "$SHARED_RAW"
125:   echo "  then: cd worktree; exec $RUNTIME $*"
126:   exit 0
127: fi
128: 
129: log "allocating worktree $WT_REL on branch $BRANCH"
130: git -C "$MAIN_ROOT" worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WT_ABS" HEAD >&2
131: 
132: # Symlink shared artifacts so the worktree reuses main's installed deps + compiled output.
133: while IFS= read -r rel; do
134:   [ -z "$rel" ] && continue
135:   src="$MAIN_ROOT/$rel"
136:   dst="$WT_ABS/$rel"
137:   if [ ! -e "$src" ]; then
138:     log "shared path absent in main, skipping: $rel"
139:     continue
140:   fi
141:   mkdir -p "$(dirname "$dst")"
142:   rm -rf "$dst"
143:   ln -s "$src" "$dst"
144:   log "linked $rel -> main"
145: done <<< "$SHARED_RAW"
146: 
147: # Per-worktree isolated MCP databases, with a short socket dir to dodge the sun_path limit.
148: mkdir -p "$WT_DB_DIR" "$WT_CG_DB_DIR" "$SOCK_DIR"
149: export SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR="$WT_DB_DIR"
150: export SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR="$WT_CG_DB_DIR"
151: export SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR="$SOCK_DIR"
152: SOCK_PATH_LEN=$(( ${#SOCK_DIR} + 16 ))
153: if [ "$SOCK_PATH_LEN" -ge 104 ]; then
154:   log "WARNING: socket path length $SOCK_PATH_LEN may exceed the platform limit (~104); the daemon could fail to listen"
155: fi
156: 
157: # Mark descendants of THIS session as children so they do not re-nest.
158: export AI_SESSION_CHILD=1
159: 
160: log "entering $WT_REL with isolated DBs; launching $RUNTIME"
161: cd "$WT_ABS"
162: exec "$RUNTIME" "$@"

(End of file - total 162 lines)
</content>

# Child detection (either signal => exec in place, no new worktree):
#   1. AI_SESSION_CHILD=1 in the environment (set at dispatch sites for orchestrated children).
#   2. Structural backstop: the process is already inside a linked git worktree
#      (git --git-dir differs from --git-common-dir).
# On an unknown/ambiguous signal we default to top-level (isolate) — the safe failure mode.

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# worktree-session.sh — launch an AI coding session in its own isolated git worktree.
#
# A top-level (human-launched) session gets its own worktree + branch + isolated MCP
# databases, so concurrent sessions on different runtimes never share a working tree or
# contend on the single-writer DB lease. An orchestrated child (subagent / dispatched
# task / deep-loop iteration) shares its parent's worktree instead of nesting.
#
# Usage:
#   worktree-session.sh <runtime> [cli-args...]
#   worktree-session.sh --dry-run <runtime> [cli-args...]   # print the plan, change nothing
#
# <runtime> is the CLI to exec (claude, codex, opencode, ...).
#
# Child detection (either signal => exec in place, no new worktree):
#   1. AI_SESSION_CHILD=1 in the environment (set at dispatch sites for orchestrated children).
#   2. Structural backstop: the process is already inside a linked git worktree
#      (git --git-dir differs from --git-common-dir).
# On an unknown/ambiguous signal we default to top-level (isolate) — the safe failure mode.
#
# DB isolation is hybrid: shared node_modules/dist are symlinked from the main checkout
# (no per-worktree reinstall/rebuild), but each worktree gets its own MCP databases via
# SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR + SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR.
#
# Socket-length workaround: the daemon's unix-domain IPC socket defaults to <db-dir>/
# daemon-ipc.sock. A worktree DB dir nested deep inside the repo blows past the platform
# sun_path limit (~104 bytes on macOS, ~108 on Linux) and the daemon dies with EINVAL on
# listen(). So we also point SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR at a short per-session dir under $HOME,
# keeping the socket path well under the limit while the DBs stay in the worktree.

set -euo pipefail

DRY_RUN=0
if [ "${1:-}" = "--dry-run" ]; then DRY_RUN=1; shift || true; fi

RUNTIME="${1:-}"
if [ -z "$RUNTIME" ]; then
  echo "usage: worktree-session.sh [--dry-run] <runtime> [cli-args...]" >&2
  exit 2
fi
shift || true

log() { echo "[worktree-session] $*" >&2; }

# Shared artifacts symlinked from main into each worktree (deps + compiled output).
# Override with SPECKIT_WORKTREE_SHARED_PATHS (newline- or colon-separated relative paths).
default_shared_paths() {
  cat <<'PATHS'
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/node_modules
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/node_modules
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/node_modules
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist
PATHS
}

exec_in_place() {
  local reason="$1"
  log "child/already-isolated session ($reason) — exec'ing in place, no new worktree"
  if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "1" ]; then
    echo "DRY_RUN: would exec in place: $RUNTIME $*"
    exit 0
  fi
  exec "$RUNTIME" "$@"
}

# --- Child detection -------------------------------------------------------
if [ "${AI_SESSION_CHILD:-}" = "1" ]; then
  exec_in_place "AI_SESSION_CHILD=1" "$@"
fi

GIT_DIR="$(git rev-parse --git-dir 2>/dev/null || true)"
GIT_COMMON_DIR="$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ -n "$GIT_DIR" ] && [ -n "$GIT_COMMON_DIR" ]; then
  # Normalize to absolute for a reliable comparison.
  GIT_DIR_ABS="$(cd "$(dirname "$GIT_DIR")" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)/$(basename "$GIT_DIR")" || GIT_DIR_ABS="$GIT_DIR"
  GIT_COMMON_ABS="$(cd "$(dirname "$GIT_COMMON_DIR")" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)/$(basename "$GIT_COMMON_DIR")" || GIT_COMMON_ABS="$GIT_COMMON_DIR"
  if [ "$GIT_DIR_ABS" != "$GIT_COMMON_ABS" ]; then
    exec_in_place "already inside a linked worktree" "$@"
  fi
fi

# --- Top-level session: allocate an isolated worktree ----------------------
MAIN_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ -z "$MAIN_ROOT" ]; then
  log "not inside a git repository — exec'ing in place (cannot isolate)"
  if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "1" ]; then echo "DRY_RUN: not a git repo; would exec in place"; exit 0; fi
  exec "$RUNTIME" "$@"
fi

# Short unique slug (epoch + pid). bash has no Date.now restriction.
SLUG="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-$$"
WT_REL=".worktrees/${RUNTIME}-${SLUG}"
WT_ABS="$MAIN_ROOT/$WT_REL"
BRANCH="work/${RUNTIME}/${SLUG}"

# Resolve the shared-artifact list.
if [ -n "${SPECKIT_WORKTREE_SHARED_PATHS:-}" ]; then
  SHARED_RAW="$(printf '%s' "$SPECKIT_WORKTREE_SHARED_PATHS" | tr ':' '\n')"
else
  SHARED_RAW="$(default_shared_paths)"
fi

WT_DB_DIR="$WT_ABS/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database"
WT_CG_DB_DIR="$WT_ABS/.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database"
# Short per-session socket dir under $HOME (NOT inside the deep worktree) to stay under the
# platform sun_path limit. The reaper cleans these alongside merged worktrees.
SOCK_DIR="$HOME/.spk-wt-sock/${RUNTIME}-${SLUG}"

if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "1" ]; then
  echo "DRY_RUN PLAN"
  echo "  main_root      = $MAIN_ROOT"
  echo "  worktree       = $WT_ABS"
  echo "  branch         = $BRANCH"
  echo "  SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR        = $WT_DB_DIR"
  echo "  SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR = $WT_CG_DB_DIR"
  echo "  SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR = $SOCK_DIR  (socket path len $(( ${#SOCK_DIR} + 16 )), platform limit ~104)"
  echo "  symlink shared paths (those present in main):"
  while IFS= read -r rel; do
    [ -z "$rel" ] && continue
    if [ -e "$MAIN_ROOT/$rel" ]; then echo "    + $rel"; else echo "    - $rel (absent in main, skip)"; fi
  done <<< "$SHARED_RAW"
  echo "  then: cd worktree; exec $RUNTIME $*"
  exit 0
fi

log "allocating worktree $WT_REL on branch $BRANCH"
git -C "$MAIN_ROOT" worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WT_ABS" HEAD >&2

# Symlink shared artifacts so the worktree reuses main's installed deps + compiled output.
while IFS= read -r rel; do
  [ -z "$rel" ] && continue
  src="$MAIN_ROOT/$rel"
  dst="$WT_ABS/$rel"
  if [ ! -e "$src" ]; then
    log "shared path absent in main, skipping: $rel"
    continue
  fi
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$dst")"
  rm -rf "$dst"
  ln -s "$src" "$dst"
  log "linked $rel -> main"
done <<< "$SHARED_RAW"

# Per-worktree isolated MCP databases, with a short socket dir to dodge the sun_path limit.
mkdir -p "$WT_DB_DIR" "$WT_CG_DB_DIR" "$SOCK_DIR"
export SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR="$WT_DB_DIR"
export SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR="$WT_CG_DB_DIR"
export SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR="$SOCK_DIR"
SOCK_PATH_LEN=$(( ${#SOCK_DIR} + 16 ))
if [ "$SOCK_PATH_LEN" -ge 104 ]; then
  log "WARNING: socket path length $SOCK_PATH_LEN may exceed the platform limit (~104); the daemon could fail to listen"
fi

# Mark descendants of THIS session as children so they do not re-nest.
export AI_SESSION_CHILD=1

log "entering $WT_REL with isolated DBs; launching $RUNTIME"
cd "$WT_ABS"
exec "$RUNTIME" "$@"

332: // Run the deep liveness probe up to `attempts` times; any 'alive' short-circuits to a bridge, and
333: // only an all-failures run returns the final (dead) result so the caller respawns. The probe fn and
334: // sleep are injectable so the retry decision is unit-testable without real sockets or timers.
335: async function probeLeaseHolderWithRetries(socketPath, options = {}) {
336:   const {
337:     probe = probeDaemon,
338:     firstTimeoutMs,
339:     retryTimeoutMs = resolveLeaseProbeRetryTimeoutMs(),
340:     retryBackoffMs = resolveLeaseProbeRetryBackoffMs(),
341:     attempts = resolveLeaseProbeAttempts(),
342:     connect,
343:     sleepFn = (ms) => new Promise((resolve) => { setTimeout(resolve, ms); }),
344:     onRetry,
345:   } = options;
346:   const totalAttempts = Math.max(1, attempts);
347:   let result = { status: 'dead', reason: 'no-probe-attempted' };
348:   for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= totalAttempts; attempt += 1) {
349:     const timeoutMs = attempt === 1 ? firstTimeoutMs : retryTimeoutMs;
350:     result = await probe(socketPath, { timeoutMs, connect, deepProbe: true });
351:     if (result.status === 'alive') return result;
352:     if (attempt < totalAttempts) {
353:       if (typeof onRetry === 'function') onRetry(attempt, totalAttempts, result);
354:       await sleepFn(retryBackoffMs);
355:     }
356:   }
357:   return result;
358: }

360: async function maybeBridgeLeaseHolder(options) {
361:   const {
362:     serviceName,
363:     leaseResult,
364:     loggerPrefix,
365:     dbDir,
366:     legacyReport,
367:     probeTimeoutMs,
368:     connect,
369:     bridge,
370:   } = options;
371:   const startedAt = leaseResult.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
372:   const legacyMarker = leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : '';
373:   const ownerPid = leaseResult.ownerPid;
374:   const writeLeaseHeld = (suffix = '') => {
375:     process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${ownerPid} startedAt=${startedAt}${legacyMarker}${suffix}\n`);
376:   };
377: 
378:   if (process.env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED === '1') {
379:     if (legacyReport) {
380:       legacyReport(leaseResult);
381:       return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
382:     }
383:     writeLeaseHeld();
384:     return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
385:   }
386: 
387:   // Prefer the path the lease owner actually recorded over recomputing one from env. Under a
388:   // divergent SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR (e.g. a secondary launcher in a different worktree env), the
389:   // recomputed path can miss the live socket and false-report 'no-bridge-socket'. Only trust the
390:   // stored path when it still exists on disk; tcp:// endpoints bypass the existence check. Fall back
391:   // to recomputation for legacy leases that predate socketPath and for the other launchers whose
392:   // leases never carry it.
393:   const storedSocketPath = leaseResult.socketPath;
394:   const usableStoredSocketPath = typeof storedSocketPath === 'string'
395:     && storedSocketPath.length > 0
396:     && (storedSocketPath.startsWith('tcp://') || fs.existsSync(storedSocketPath))
397:     ? storedSocketPath
398:     : null;
399:   const socketPath = usableStoredSocketPath ?? getIpcSocketPath(serviceName, { dbDir });
400:   if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://') && !fs.existsSync(socketPath)) {
401:     writeLeaseHeld(' (no-bridge-socket)');
402:     return { action: 'report', reason: 'no-bridge-socket', socketPath };
403:   }
404: 
405:   // deepProbe: REQUIRE a JSON-RPC reply, not just a socket accept. The reap/bridge decision must
406:   // detect a HUNG daemon (event loop wedged / deadlocked — it still accepts the UDS connection but
407:   // never services requests). A connect-ok-only probe would bridge a client to the wedged daemon and
408:   // hang it forever, never respawning. The raised probe timeout (default 5000ms, < the 7000ms launcher
409:   // grace) already prevents false-reaping a busy-but-responsive daemon mid-FTS-merge, so deep probing
410:   // does not regress that.
411:   // Require N consecutive failures (not one transient miss) before reaping a sibling's owner.
412:   const probeAttempts = resolveLeaseProbeAttempts();
413:   const probe = await probeLeaseHolderWithRetries(socketPath, {
414:     firstTimeoutMs: probeTimeoutMs,
415:     attempts: probeAttempts,
416:     connect,
417:     onRetry: (attempt, total, result) => process.stderr.write(
418:       `[${loggerPrefix}] lease holder pid=${ownerPid} probe ${attempt}/${total} not alive (${result.reason}); retrying\n`,
419:     ),
420:   });
421:   if (probe.status !== 'alive') {
422:     process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath} failed ${probeAttempts} consecutive liveness probes: ${probe.reason}\n`);
423:     return { action: 'respawn', reason: probe.reason, socketPath };
424:   }
425: 
426:   process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] bridging to lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath}\n`);
427:   const bridgeToSocket = bridge ?? bridgeStdioToSocket;
428:   // Await so an injected reconnecting bridge (whose start() resolves after the first
429:   // attach) is fully wired before returning. The raw bridge returns a socket, not a
430:   // promise, so Promise.resolve keeps the original fire-and-forget timing for it.
431:   await Promise.resolve(bridgeToSocket(socketPath, {
432:     onError: () => {
433:       writeLeaseHeld(' (bridge-refused)');
434:     },
435:   }));
436:   return { action: 'bridge', socketPath };
437: }
438: 
439: module.exports = {
440:   DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
441:   bridgeStdioToSocket,
442:   getIpcSocketPath,
443:   maybeBridgeLeaseHolder,
444:   probeDaemon,
445:   probeLeaseHolderWithRetries,
446:   probeModelServer,
447:   resolveLeaseProbeAttempts,
448:   resolveLeaseProbeRetryBackoffMs,
449:   resolveLeaseProbeRetryTimeoutMs,
450:   toConnectionOptions,
451: };

459: function assertSunPathLimit(socketPath) {

464:   if (byteLength <= 103) return;

466:     'ESUNPATHTOOLONG',

471: function assertSocketDirOwnership(socketPath, options = {}) {

475:   const socketDir = path.dirname(socketPath);

477:   try {
478:     dirStat = statApi.lstatSync(socketDir);
479:   } catch (error) {
480:     if (error.code !== 'ENOENT') throw error;
481:   }
482: 
483:   if (dirStat) {
484:     if (dirStat.isSymbolicLink()) {
485:       throw codedError('ESOCKETDIRSYMLINK', `Refusing to use symlinked SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR for hf-model-server socket: ${socketDir}`);
486:     }

490:     }
491:   }

493:   try {
494:     const socketStat = statApi.lstatSync(socketPath);

501: }

601: // ── Maintenance-active marker ────────────────────────────────────────────────
602: // A background index scan can legitimately keep the daemon's event loop busy for
603: // stretches, so a competing launcher's deep liveness probe sees an unresponsive
604: // socket and would reap+respawn the daemon mid-scan (killing the scan). The
605: // daemon writes this marker beside its DB and refreshes it on a timer; the
606: // launcher uses it to tell "busy-by-design" from "wedged": a fresh marker naming
607: // the live daemon child means ADOPT, not reap. A genuinely wedged daemon cannot
608: // fire its refresh timer, so the marker expires and normal reaping resumes.
609: const MAINTENANCE_MARKER_FILE_NAME = '.maintenance-active.json';

611: function maintenanceMarkerPath(dbDir) {
612:   return path.join(dbDir, MAINTENANCE_MARKER_FILE_NAME);
613: }

615: function readMaintenanceMarker(dbDir, options = {}) {

617:   try {
618:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fsApi.readFileSync(maintenanceMarkerPath(dbDir), 'utf8'));
619:     if (Number.isInteger(parsed?.childPid) && parsed.childPid > 0 && Number.isFinite(parsed?.activeUntilMs)) {
620:       return parsed;
621:     }
622:   } catch {
623:     // Missing or corrupt marker => treat as no active maintenance.
624:   }
625:   return null;
626: }

628: // Pure adopt-vs-reap decision. Returns true only when a fresh marker names this
629: // exact live child. Fail-safe toward reaping: a missing/expired marker, a
630: // childPid mismatch, or a non-'alive' child all fall through to the normal reap
631: // path so a stale marker can never pin a genuinely dead or wedged daemon.
632: function shouldAdoptDespiteProbe(options = {}) {
633:   const { marker, childPid, childLiveness } = options;
634:   if (!marker) return false;
635:   if (!Number.isInteger(childPid) || childPid <= 0) return false;
636:   if (marker.childPid !== childPid) return false;
637:   if (!(Number.isFinite(marker.activeUntilMs) && marker.activeUntilMs > nowMsFromOptions(options))) return false;
638:   if (childLiveness !== 'alive') return false;
639:   return true;
640: }

642: function isRespawnLockStale(raw, options = {}) {
643:   const liveness = options.liveness || processLiveness;
644:   const nowMs = typeof options.nowMs === 'number' ? options.nowMs : Date.now();
645:   const staleMs = typeof options.staleMs === 'number' ? options.staleMs : RESPAWN_LOCK_STALE_MS;
646:   let parsed;
647:   try {
648:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
649:   } catch {
650:     return true;
651:   }
652:   const pid = parsed && parsed.pid;
653:   const hasPid = Number.isInteger(pid) && pid > 0;
654:   if (hasPid && liveness(pid) === 'dead') return true;
655:   // A live demand listener deliberately holds this lock across its whole bind + idle-listener
656:   // window, which can exceed RESPAWN_LOCK_STALE_MS. Bound staleness to listener liveness: only let the
657:   // wall-clock age expire the lock when the recorded owner is NOT confirmably alive (absent/invalid pid,
658:   // or an EPERM-opaque pid we cannot prove is ours). If the owner pid is alive, it still owns the lock —
659:   // never reclaim it from under a live owner on age alone.
660:   if (hasPid && liveness(pid) === 'alive') return false;
661:   const startedAtMs = parsed && parsed.startedAt ? Date.parse(parsed.startedAt) : NaN;
662:   if (Number.isFinite(startedAtMs) && nowMs - startedAtMs > staleMs) return true;
663:   return false;
664: }

666: function acquireRespawnLockFileAt(lockPath, label = 'respawn', options = {}) {
667:   const logger = options.log || defaultLog;
668:   const rel = options.rel || ((p) => p);
669:   const fsApi = { ...fs, ...(options.fs || {}) };
670:   const currentLockPath = path.join(ensureCanonicalDir(path.dirname(lockPath)), path.basename(lockPath));
671:   const tryOpen = () => {
672:     let fd = null;
673:     try {
674:       fd = fsApi.openSync(currentLockPath, 'wx', 0o600);
675:       fsApi.writeFileSync(fd, `${JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, startedAt: new Date().toISOString() }, null, 2)}\n`, 'utf8');
676:       fsApi.fsyncSync(fd);
677:       return { acquired: true, fd, path: currentLockPath };
678:     } catch (error) {
679:       if (typeof fd === 'number') {
680:         try {
681:           fsApi.closeSync(fd);
682:         } catch {
683:           // Close failure is secondary to the durable-write failure.
684:         }
685:         cleanupTmpFile(currentLockPath, fsApi);
686:       }
687:       if (isDurableWriteUnavailable(error)) {
688:         logDurableWriteUnavailableOnce(logger, `${label} lock acquire`, currentLockPath, error, rel);
689:         return { acquired: false, path: currentLockPath, reason: 'durable-write-unavailable' };
690:       }
691:       throw error;
692:     }
693:   };
694:   try {
695:     const opened = tryOpen();
696:     if (!opened.acquired) return opened;
697:     return opened;
698:   } catch (error) {
699:     if (error.code !== 'EEXIST') throw error;
700:   }
701:   let raw = '';
702:   try {
703:     raw = fsApi.readFileSync(currentLockPath, 'utf8');
704:   } catch (readErr) {
705:     if (readErr.code !== 'ENOENT') throw readErr;
706:   }
707:   if (raw === '' || isRespawnLockStale(raw, options)) {
708:     // Atomically CLAIM the stale lock via rename before deleting it, then re-open
709:     // exclusively. A bare unlink+open is not mutually exclusive: two racers can
710:     // interleave (A unlink, A open/holds, B unlink removes A's fresh lock, B
711:     // open/holds) and both believe they hold the exclusive lock. Renaming a path
712:     // only one racer can win serializes the reclaim — a loser's rename throws
713:     // ENOENT and falls through to return acquired:false.
714:     const staleClaim = `${currentLockPath}.stale.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}`;
715:     try {
716:       fsApi.renameSync(currentLockPath, staleClaim);
717:     } catch (renameErr) {
718:       if (renameErr.code === 'ENOENT') return { acquired: false, path: currentLockPath };
719:       throw renameErr;
720:     }
721:     try {
722:       fsApi.unlinkSync(staleClaim);
723:     } catch (unlinkErr) {
724:       if (unlinkErr.code !== 'ENOENT') throw unlinkErr;
725:     }
726:     try {
727:       const reclaimed = tryOpen();
728:       if (!reclaimed.acquired) return reclaimed;
729:       logger(`reclaimed stale ${label} lock ${rel(currentLockPath)}`);
730:       return reclaimed;
731:     } catch (retryErr) {
732:       if (retryErr.code !== 'EEXIST') throw retryErr;
733:     }
734:   }
735:   return { acquired: false, path: currentLockPath };
736: }

750:   } finally {

753:     } catch (error) {
754:       if (error.code !== 'ENOENT') throw error;

756:   }
757: }

759: function buildLeaseObject(childPid = null, startedAt = null, modelServerPid = null, socketPath = null) {

799: function createModelServerSupervisor(options = {}) {

842:   function reapProcessTree(childPid) {

850:   async function reapBeforeRespawn(childPid) {

854:     }
855:     if (pidState === 'dead') {
856:       return { allowed: true, reaped: false, reason: 'model-server-already-dead' };
857:     }

859:     logger(`confirmed-dead hf-embed socket; reaping recorded hf-model-server pid ${childPid} before respawn`);
860:     signal(childPid, 'SIGTERM');
861:     reapProcessTree(childPid);
862:     const exitedAfterTerm = await waitForExit(childPid, RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS);
863:     if (!exitedAfterTerm) {
864:       logger(`hf-model-server pid ${childPid} exceeded ${RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS}ms reap grace; sending SIGKILL`);
865:       signal(childPid, 'SIGKILL');

871:   function scheduleDemandListener() {

878:   function launch() {

903:       runner: processRowsRunner,

909:         },
910:       },
911:       snapshot: {

915:       },

951:     return true;
952:   }
953: 
954:   return {
955:     clearTimers,
956:     getChild: () => state.child,
957:     getPid,
958:     getSnapshotPids: () => [...state.lastKnownDescendantPids],

960:     reapBeforeRespawn,
961:     reapProcessTree,

965: function createModelServerControl(deps = {}) {

<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
3: // ║ COMPONENT: HF Model Server                                                ║
4: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
5: // ║ PURPOSE: Local HTTP/UDS embedding server for HuggingFace transformers      ║
6: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
7: 
8: 'use strict';
9: 
10: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11: // 1. IMPORTS
12: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
13: 
14: const fs = require('fs');
15: const http = require('http');
16: const net = require('net');
17: const path = require('path');
18: const { createRequire } = require('module');
19: const { pathToFileURL } = require('url');
20: 
21: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22: // 2. CONSTANTS
23: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: 
25: const SOCKET_FILE_NAME = 'hf-embed.sock';
26: const DEFAULT_MODEL = 'nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5';
27: const DEFAULT_DTYPE = 'q8';
28: const DEFAULT_INFERENCE_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000;
29: const MODEL_LOAD_TIMEOUT = 120000;
30: const INFERENCE_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000;
31: const ALLOWED_HF_LOCAL_DTYPES = Object.freeze([
32:   'fp32',
33:   'fp16',
34:   'q4',
35:   'q4f16',
36:   'q8',
37:   'int8',
38:   'uint8',
39:   'bnb4',
40: ]);
41: const HEALTH_PATH = '/api/health';
42: const EMBED_PATH = '/api/embed';
43: const MAX_REQUEST_BYTES = 1024 * 1024;
44: const DEFAULT_SELF_WARM_INPUT = 'test warmup query';
45: // Loopback hosts we permit binding without an explicit auth token. Any other host
46: // exposes the embedding server (which loads + runs arbitrary model inference on
47: // caller-supplied text) to the network, so it is refused unless the operator opts
48: // in via HF_EMBED_ALLOW_REMOTE_BIND=1 AND supplies HF_EMBED_AUTH_TOKEN.
49: const LOOPBACK_BIND_HOSTS = Object.freeze(['127.0.0.1', '::1', 'localhost']);
50: // How long the perimeter live-resident probe waits for a UDS connection before
51: // concluding the socket is stale. Bounded so a hung peer cannot wedge startup.
52: const SOCKET_RESIDENT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS = 250;
53: 
54: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55: // 3. PATH AND TRANSPORT HELPERS
56: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
57: 
58: function repoRoot() {
59:   return path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '..');
60: }
61: 
62: function systemSpecKitRoot() {
63:   return path.join(repoRoot(), '.opencode', 'skills', 'system-spec-kit');
64: }
65: 
66: function defaultDbDir() {
67:   return path.join(systemSpecKitRoot(), 'mcp_server', 'database');
68: }
69: 
70: function normalizeProfileDtype(value) {
71:   const normalized = value?.trim().toLowerCase();
72:   if (!normalized) {
73:     return null;
74:   }
75:   return normalized.replace(/[^a-z0-9-_.]/g, '_').replace(/__+/g, '_');
76: }
77: 
78: function resolveDtype(explicit) {
79:   const raw = explicit ?? process.env.HF_EMBEDDINGS_DTYPE ?? DEFAULT_DTYPE;
80:   const normalized = normalizeProfileDtype(raw);
81:   if (!normalized || !ALLOWED_HF_LOCAL_DTYPES.includes(normalized)) {
82:     console.warn(
83:       `[hf-model-server] Unknown HF_EMBEDDINGS_DTYPE="${raw}"; falling back to ${DEFAULT_DTYPE}. Allowed: ${ALLOWED_HF_LOCAL_DTYPES.join(', ')}`,
84:     );
85:     return DEFAULT_DTYPE;
86:   }
87:   return normalized;
88: }
89: 
90: function normalizeListenTarget(value) {
91:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim() === '') {
92:     return null;
93:   }
94:   const raw = value.trim();
95:   if (raw.startsWith('tcp://')) {
96:     return raw;
97:   }
98:   if (raw.startsWith('http://') || raw.startsWith('https://')) {
99:     const url = new URL(raw);
100:     return `tcp://${url.hostname}:${url.port || (url.protocol === 'https:' ? '443' : '80')}`;
101:   }
102:   if (raw.startsWith('unix://')) {
103:     return path.resolve(raw.slice('unix://'.length));
104:   }
105:   return path.resolve(raw);
106: }
107: 
108: function resolveListenTarget(options = {}) {
109:   const env = options.env ?? process.env;
110:   const explicitTarget = normalizeListenTarget(options.listenTarget ?? env.HF_EMBED_SERVER_URL);
111:   if (explicitTarget) {
112:     return explicitTarget;
113:   }
114:   if (env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR?.startsWith('tcp://')) {
115:     return env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR;
116:   }
117: 
118:   const socketDir = env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR
119:     ? path.resolve(env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR)
120:     : path.resolve(options.dbDir ?? defaultDbDir());
121:   return path.join(socketDir, SOCKET_FILE_NAME);
122: }
123: 
124: function toConnectionOptions(socketPath) {
125:   if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
126:     return socketPath;
127:   }
128:   const url = new URL(socketPath);
129:   return {
130:     host: url.hostname,
131:     port: Number.parseInt(url.port, 10),
132:   };
133: }
134: 
135: function getListeningEndpoint(server, target) {
136:   const address = server.address();
137:   if (address && typeof address === 'object') {
138:     return `tcp://${address.address}:${address.port}`;
139:   }
140:   return target;
141: }
142: 
143: function isLoopbackHost(host) {
144:   if (typeof host !== 'string' || host.trim() === '') {
145:     // An empty/missing host defaults to the loopback bind in listenOnce, so it is safe.
146:     return true;
147:   }
148:   return LOOPBACK_BIND_HOSTS.includes(host.trim().toLowerCase());
149: }
150: 
151: // A tcp:// target takes its host verbatim from the operator URL. Binding a
152: // non-loopback host would expose model inference to the network. Refuse unless the operator
153: // explicitly opts into a remote bind AND provides an auth token. Pure host/env check so it is
154: // deterministically testable without opening a socket.
155: function assertLoopbackBindAllowed(target, options = {}) {
156:   if (typeof target !== 'string' || !target.startsWith('tcp://')) {
157:     return;
158:   }
159:   const url = new URL(target);
160:   const host = url.hostname;
161:   if (isLoopbackHost(host)) {
162:     return;
163:   }
164:   const env = options.env ?? process.env;
165:   const allowRemote = env.HF_EMBED_ALLOW_REMOTE_BIND === '1' || env.HF_EMBED_ALLOW_REMOTE_BIND === 'true';
166:   const authToken = typeof env.HF_EMBED_AUTH_TOKEN === 'string' ? env.HF_EMBED_AUTH_TOKEN.trim() : '';
167:   if (allowRemote && authToken.length > 0) {
168:     return;
169:   }
170:   const error = new Error(
171:     `[hf-model-server] Refusing to bind non-loopback host "${host}" for ${target}. ` +
172:     'The embedding server has no transport auth; binding a routable interface would expose ' +
173:     'model inference to the network. Use a loopback host (127.0.0.1/::1/localhost), or set ' +
174:     'HF_EMBED_ALLOW_REMOTE_BIND=1 together with a non-empty HF_EMBED_AUTH_TOKEN to opt in.',
175:   );
176:   error.code = 'EPERM_NONLOOPBACK_BIND';
177:   throw error;
178: }
179: 
180: // Perimeter guard: assert the socket directory is owned by the current
181: // uid and is not group/world-writable before we are willing to unlink a path inside it. Mirrors
182: // the supervised path's assertSocketDirOwnership intent so the direct-startup unlink cannot be
183: // tricked into deleting a file in an attacker-controlled directory.
184: function assertSocketDirOwnership(target, options = {}) {
185:   const fsImpl = options.fs ?? fs;
186:   // process.getuid is undefined on platforms without POSIX uids (e.g. Windows); skip there.
187:   const getuid = options.getuid ?? (typeof process.getuid === 'function' ? () => process.getuid() : null);
188:   if (!getuid) {
189:     return;
190:   }
191:   const dir = path.dirname(target);
192:   let stat;
193:   try {
194:     stat = fsImpl.statSync(dir);
195:   } catch (error) {
196:     if (error && error.code === 'ENOENT') {
197:       // Directory does not exist yet — nothing resident to protect; mkdir happens upstream.
198:       return;
199:     }
200:     throw error;
201:   }
202:   const currentUid = getuid();
203:   if (stat.uid !== currentUid) {
204:     const error = new Error(
205:       `[hf-model-server] Refusing to reclaim socket "${target}": directory ${dir} is owned by ` +
206:       `uid ${stat.uid}, not the current uid ${currentUid}.`,
207:     );
208:     error.code = 'EPERM_SOCKET_DIR_OWNER';
209:     throw error;
210:   }
211:   // 0o022 == group-write | other-write. A writable-by-others socket dir means another user could
212:   // have planted the resident socket, so unlinking it is unsafe.
213:   if ((stat.mode & 0o022) !== 0) {
214:     const error = new Error(
215:       `[hf-model-server] Refusing to reclaim socket "${target}": directory ${dir} is ` +
216:       `group/world-writable (mode ${(stat.mode & 0o777).toString(8)}).`,
217:     );
218:     error.code = 'EPERM_SOCKET_DIR_PERMS';
219:     throw error;
220:   }
221: }
222: 
223: // Perimeter guard: probe whether a live process is still listening on the
224: // UDS before unlinking it. If a peer accepts the connection the socket is live-resident and we MUST
225: // NOT unlink it (that would orphan a healthy server and let two servers race the same path); we
226: // re-surface EADDRINUSE instead. ECONNREFUSED/ENOENT means the socket is stale and safe to reclaim.
227: // Injectable connector keeps this deterministic in tests (no real socket required).
228: async function probeSocketResident(target, options = {}) {
229:   const timeoutMs = Number.isFinite(options.probeTimeoutMs)
230:     ? options.probeTimeoutMs
231:     : SOCKET_RESIDENT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS;
232:   const connect = options.connect
233:     ?? ((connectOptions, onConnect) => net.connect(connectOptions, onConnect));
234:   return await new Promise((resolve) => {
235:     let settled = false;
236:     let socket;
237:     const finish = (isResident) => {
238:       if (settled) {
239:         return;
240:       }
241:       settled = true;
242:       try {
243:         socket?.destroy();
244:       } catch {
245:         // best-effort teardown
246:       }
247:       resolve(isResident);
248:     };
249:     try {
250:       socket = connect({ path: target }, () => finish(true));
251:     } catch {
252:       // Synchronous connect failure (e.g. ENOENT) => stale.
253:       finish(false);
254:       return;
255:     }
256:     if (socket && typeof socket.once === 'function') {
257:       socket.once('error', () => finish(false));
258:       if (typeof socket.setTimeout === 'function') {
259:         socket.setTimeout(timeoutMs, () => finish(false));
260:       }
261:     }
262:   });
263: }
264: 
265: async function listenOnce(server, target, options = {}) {
266:   await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
267:     const onError = (error) => {
268:       server.off('listening', onListening);
269:       reject(error);
270:     };
271:     const onListening = () => {
272:       server.off('error', onError);
273:       resolve();
274:     };
275:     server.once('error', onError);
276:     server.once('listening', onListening);
277:     if (target.startsWith('tcp://')) {
278:       let url;
279:       try {
280:         assertLoopbackBindAllowed(target, options);
281:         url = new URL(target);
282:       } catch (error) {
283:         server.off('listening', onListening);
284:         server.off('error', onError);
285:         reject(error);
286:         return;
287:       }
288:       server.listen(Number.parseInt(url.port, 10), url.hostname || '127.0.0.1');
289:       return;
290:     }
291:     server.listen(target);
292:   });
293: }
294: 
295: async function listenHttpServer(server, target, options = {}) {
296:   // Filesystem side-effects go through an injectable fs so the EADDRINUSE/perimeter path is
297:   // fully testable without touching disk; production leaves options.fs undefined => real fs.
298:   const fsImpl = options.fs ?? fs;
299:   if (!target.startsWith('tcp://')) {
300:     fsImpl.mkdirSync(path.dirname(target), { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
301:     // mkdirSync's mode only applies when it creates the dir — a pre-existing
302:     // group/world-writable socket dir keeps its loose perms. Enforce the uid/perm
303:     // invariant on first bind too, not only on the EADDRINUSE reclaim path below.
304:     assertSocketDirOwnership(target, options);
305:   }
306: 
307:   try {
308:     await listenOnce(server, target, options);
309:   } catch (error) {
310:     if (error.code !== 'EADDRINUSE' || target.startsWith('tcp://')) {
311:       throw error;
312:     }
313:     // Apply the same perimeter guard the supervised path uses before unlinking a
314:     // resident UDS on the direct-startup path. (1) the dir must be ours and not other-writable;
315:     // (2) if a live process still answers on the socket, refuse to unlink and re-surface the
316:     // address-in-use error rather than orphaning a healthy server.
317:     assertSocketDirOwnership(target, options);
318:     const resident = await probeSocketResident(target, options);
319:     if (resident) {
320:       const liveError = new Error(
321:         `[hf-model-server] Socket ${target} is in use by a live resident process; refusing to unlink it.`,
322:       );
323:       liveError.code = 'EADDRINUSE';
324:       throw liveError;
325:     }
326:     try {
327:       fsImpl.unlinkSync(target);
328:     } catch (unlinkError) {
329:       if (unlinkError.code !== 'ENOENT') {
330:         throw unlinkError;
331:       }
332:     }
333:     await listenOnce(server, target, options);
334:   }
335: 
336:   if (!target.startsWith('tcp://')) {
337:     fsImpl.chmodSync(target, 0o600);
338:   }
339: 
340:   return getListeningEndpoint(server, target);
341: }

343: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
344: // 4. MODEL LOAD HELPERS
345: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
346: 
347: function getOptimalDevice() {
348:   if (process.platform === 'darwin') {
349:     return 'mps';
350:   }
351:   return 'cpu';
352: }
353: 
354: function getErrorMessage(error) {
355:   return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
356: }
357: 
358: function getErrorCode(error) {
359:   if (typeof error !== 'object' || error === null || !('code' in error)) {
360:     return undefined;
361:   }
362:   const code = error.code;
363:   return typeof code === 'string' ? code : undefined;
364: }
365: 
366: function createTimeoutError(message) {
367:   const error = new Error(message);
368:   error.code = 'ETIMEDOUT';
369:   return error;
370: }
371: 
372: function getSessionCount(sessions) {
373:   if (!sessions) {
374:     return 0;
375:   }
376:   if (sessions instanceof Map) {
377:     return sessions.size;
378:   }
379:   if (Array.isArray(sessions)) {
380:     return sessions.length;
381:   }
382:   return Object.keys(sessions).length;
383: }
384: 
385: async function withTimeout(operation, timeoutMs, message) {
386:   return await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
387:     const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => reject(createTimeoutError(message)), timeoutMs);
388: 
389:     operation
390:       .then((value) => {
391:         clearTimeout(timeoutId);
392:         resolve(value);
393:       })
394:       .catch((error) => {
395:         clearTimeout(timeoutId);
396:         reject(error);
397:       });
398:   });
399: }
400: 
401: async function importTransformers() {
402:   try {
403:     return await import('@huggingface/transformers');
404:   } catch (error) {
405:     if (getErrorCode(error) !== 'ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND' || !getErrorMessage(error).includes('@huggingface/transformers')) {
406:       throw error;
407:     }
408:     const packageRequire = createRequire(path.join(systemSpecKitRoot(), 'package.json'));
409:     const resolved = packageRequire.resolve('@huggingface/transformers');
410:     return await import(pathToFileURL(resolved).href);
411:   }
412: }
413: 
414: async function loadHfModel(options = {}) {
415:   const modelName = options.modelName || process.env.HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL || DEFAULT_MODEL;
416:   const dtype = options.dtype || resolveDtype();
417:   const importer = options.importTransformers || importTransformers;
418:   const timeoutMs = options.modelLoadTimeoutMs || MODEL_LOAD_TIMEOUT;
419:   const onLoadAttemptStart = typeof options.onLoadAttemptStart === 'function'
420:     ? options.onLoadAttemptStart
421:     : null;
422:   const start = Date.now();
423: 
424:   try {
425:     console.warn(`[hf-model-server] Loading ${modelName} (dtype=${dtype}, first load may take 15-30s)...`);
426: 
427:     const { pipeline } = await importer();
428: 
429:     let targetDevice = getOptimalDevice();
430:     console.error(`[hf-model-server] Attempting device: ${targetDevice}`);
431: 
432:     const loadWithTimeout = async (device) => {
433:       const attemptStartedAt = Date.now();
434:       onLoadAttemptStart?.({ device, startedAt: attemptStartedAt, timeoutMs });
435:       return await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
436:         const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => {
437:           reject(new Error(`Model loading timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms. ` +
438:             'This may indicate a corrupted cache or network issue. ' +
439:             'Try clearing: ~/.cache/huggingface/hub/'));
440:         }, timeoutMs);
441: 
442:         pipeline('feature-extraction', modelName, {
443:           dtype,
444:           device,
445:         }).then((extractor) => {
446:           clearTimeout(timeoutId);
447:           resolve(extractor);
448:         }).catch((error) => {
449:           clearTimeout(timeoutId);
450:           reject(error);
451:         });
452:       });
453:     };
454: 
455:     let extractor;
456:     let currentDevice;
457:     try {
458:       extractor = await loadWithTimeout(targetDevice);
459:       currentDevice = targetDevice;
460:     } catch (deviceError) {
461:       if (targetDevice !== 'cpu' && !getErrorMessage(deviceError).includes('timed out')) {
462:         console.warn(`[hf-model-server] ${targetDevice.toUpperCase()} unavailable (${getErrorMessage(deviceError)}), using CPU`);
463:         extractor = await loadWithTimeout('cpu');
464:         currentDevice = 'cpu';
465:       } else {
466:         throw deviceError;
467:       }
468:     }
469: 
470:     const loadTimeMs = Date.now() - start;
471:     console.warn(`[hf-model-server] Model loaded in ${loadTimeMs}ms (device: ${currentDevice})`);
472: 
473:     return {
474:       extractor,
475:       device: currentDevice,
476:       loadTimeMs,
477:     };
478:   } catch (error) {
479:     const errMsg = getErrorMessage(error);
480:     const errCode = getErrorCode(error);
481:     if (
482:       errCode === 'ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED' ||
483:       errMsg.includes('NODE_MODULE_VERSION') ||
484:       errMsg.includes('was compiled against a different Node.js version')
485:     ) {
486:       console.error('[hf-model-server] ═══ NATIVE MODULE ERROR ═══');
487:       console.error(`[hf-model-server] ${errMsg}`);
488:       console.error(`[hf-model-server] Running: Node ${process.version} (MODULE_VERSION ${process.versions.modules})`);
489:       console.error('[hf-model-server] Recovery options:');
490:       console.error('[hf-model-server]   1. Clear cache: rm -rf ~/.cache/huggingface/hub/');
491:       console.error('[hf-model-server]   2. Switch provider: EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER=voyage');
492:       console.error('[hf-model-server] ═══════════════════════════');
493:     }
494: 
495:     throw error;
496:   }
497: }

499: function normalizeLoadResult(result, fallbackLoadTimeMs) {
500:   if (result && typeof result === 'object' && Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(result, 'extractor')) {
501:     return {
502:       extractor: result.extractor,
503:       device: result.device ?? null,
504:       loadTimeMs: result.loadTimeMs ?? fallbackLoadTimeMs,
505:     };
506:   }
507:   return {
508:     extractor: result,
509:     device: null,
510:     loadTimeMs: fallbackLoadTimeMs,
511:   };
512: }
513: 
514: function extractorRunner(extractor) {
515:   if (typeof extractor === 'function') {
516:     return extractor;
517:   }
518:   if (extractor && typeof extractor.embed === 'function') {
519:     return extractor.embed.bind(extractor);
520:   }
521:   throw new Error('[hf-model-server] Loaded extractor is not callable');
522: }
523: 
524: function embeddingData(output) {
525:   const data = output && typeof output === 'object' && Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(output, 'data')
526:     ? output.data
527:     : output;
528:   if (data instanceof Float32Array) {
529:     return data;
530:   }
531:   if (Array.isArray(data)) {
532:     return new Float32Array(data);
533:   }
534:   throw new Error('[hf-model-server] Extractor output does not contain embedding data');
535: }
536: 
537: function assertSingleSessionBeforeDispose(extractor) {
538:   const sessionCount = getSessionCount(extractor?.model?.sessions);
539:   if (sessionCount !== 1) {
540:     throw new Error(`[hf-model-server] Expected exactly one native session before dispose, got ${sessionCount}`);
541:   }
542:   if (typeof extractor.dispose !== 'function') {
543:     throw new Error('[hf-model-server] Loaded extractor does not expose dispose()');
544:   }
545: }
