547: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
548: // 5. SERVER FACTORY
549: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
550: 
551: function createHfModelServer(options = {}) {
552:   const state = {
553:     modelName: options.modelName || process.env.HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL || DEFAULT_MODEL,
554:     dtype: resolveDtype(options.dtype),
555:     inferenceTimeoutMs: options.inferenceTimeoutMs || DEFAULT_INFERENCE_TIMEOUT_MS,
556:     extractor: null,
557:     loadingPromise: null,
558:     disposePromise: null,
559:     inFlightRawRuns: new Set(),
560:     recentInferMs: [],
561:     inferenceCount: 0,
562:     lastInferenceMs: null,
563:     supportsArrayInput: undefined,
564:     serverState: 'loading',
565:     loadStartedAt: null,
566:     loadProgressAt: null,
567:     lastSuccessfulEmbedAt: null,
568:     device: null,
569:     dim: 0,
570:     loadTimeMs: null,
571:     lastError: null,
572:     listenTarget: null,
573:     listenedEndpoint: null,
574:     isDisposed: false,
575:   };
576:   const loadModel = options.loadModel || loadHfModel;
577:   const listenServer = options.listen || listenHttpServer;
578:   // Perimeter/loopback guard injection points. Tests inject
579:   // connect/fs/getuid/env/probeTimeoutMs to exercise the guard deterministically without real
580:   // sockets or filesystem ownership. Production leaves these undefined => real net/fs/process/env.
581:   const listenGuardOptions = {
582:     env: options.env,
583:     fs: options.fs,
584:     getuid: options.getuid,
585:     connect: options.connect,
586:     probeTimeoutMs: options.probeTimeoutMs,
587:   };
588:   const selfWarmInput = options.selfWarmInput || DEFAULT_SELF_WARM_INPUT;
589:   const shouldSelfWarm = options.selfWarm !== false;
590:   const logger = options.logger || console;
591:   const activeSockets = new Set();
592: 
593:   function recordInferenceMs(inferMs) {
594:     state.recentInferMs.push(inferMs);
595:     if (state.recentInferMs.length > 256) {
596:       state.recentInferMs.shift();
597:     }
598:     state.inferenceCount += 1;
599:     state.lastInferenceMs = inferMs;
600:   }
601: 
602:   function percentile(sortedValues, percentileValue) {
603:     const index = Math.min(
604:       sortedValues.length - 1,
605:       Math.ceil((percentileValue / 100) * sortedValues.length) - 1,
606:     );
607:     return sortedValues[index] ?? null;
608:   }
609: 
610:   function timingSummary() {
611:     if (state.recentInferMs.length === 0) {
612:       return {
613:         p50Ms: null,
614:         p95Ms: null,
615:         lastMs: null,
616:         count: 0,
617:       };
618:     }
619: 
620:     const sorted = state.recentInferMs.slice().sort((left, right) => left - right);
621:     return {
622:       p50Ms: percentile(sorted, 50),
623:       p95Ms: percentile(sorted, 95),
624:       lastMs: state.lastInferenceMs,
625:       count: state.inferenceCount,
626:     };
627:   }
628: 
629:   function isArrayInputUnsupported(error) {
630:     // A transient connection/timeout error is NOT a capability gap — it is retryable and must
631:     // never permanently latch batching off (mirrors hf-local.ts isRetryableReadinessError).
632:     // Without this guard a one-off first-batch ETIMEDOUT/ECONNRESET silently degrades the
633:     // server to per-row inference for its whole lifetime.
634:     const code = getErrorCode(error);
635:     if (code === 'ETIMEDOUT' || code === 'ECONNRESET' || code === 'EPIPE' || code === 'ECONNREFUSED' || code === 'ECONNABORTED') {
636:       return false;
637:     }
638:     // Only an unambiguous "this runner rejects array input" message latches batching off. A bare
639:     // TypeError or a generic "shape"/"invalid input" error is not a reliable capability signal.
640:     return /array input|unsupported array input|input must be a string|expected (?:a )?string|got an array|received an array/i.test(getErrorMessage(error));
641:   }
642: 
643:   async function runExtractor(extractor, text) {
644:     const t0 = performance.now();
645:     const run = Promise.resolve(extractorRunner(extractor)(text, {
646:       pooling: 'mean',
647:       normalize: true,
648:     }));
649:     state.inFlightRawRuns.add(run);
650:     void run.finally(() => {
651:       state.inFlightRawRuns.delete(run);
652:     }).catch(() => undefined);
653: 
654:     const output = await withTimeout(
655:       run,
656:       state.inferenceTimeoutMs,
657:       `HF local inference timed out after ${state.inferenceTimeoutMs}ms`,
658:     );
659:     recordInferenceMs(performance.now() - t0);
660:     const embedding = embeddingData(output);
661:     if (state.dim <= 0) {
662:       state.dim = embedding.length;
663:     } else if (embedding.length !== state.dim) {
664:       throw new Error(`Embedding dimension mismatch: expected ${state.dim}, got ${embedding.length}`);
665:     }
666:     return Array.from(embedding);
667:   }
668: 
669:   function sliceBatchOutput(output, n) {
670:     const data = embeddingData(output);
671:     if (output && typeof output === 'object' && Array.isArray(output.dims)) {
672:       const [rows, dim] = output.dims;
673:       if (rows !== n || dim * n !== data.length) {
674:         throw new Error(`[hf-model-server] Batch output dimensions mismatch: dims=${JSON.stringify(output.dims)} data=${data.length} inputs=${n}`);
675:       }
676:     }
677: 
678:     if (n <= 0) {
679:       return [];
680:     }
681:     if (data.length % n !== 0) {
682:       throw new Error(`[hf-model-server] Batch output length ${data.length} is not divisible by input count ${n}`);
683:     }
684: 
685:     const dim = data.length / n;
686:     if (!Number.isInteger(dim)) {
687:       throw new Error(`[hf-model-server] Batch output dimension is not an integer: ${dim}`);
688:     }
689:     if (state.dim <= 0) {
690:       state.dim = dim;
691:     } else if (dim !== state.dim) {
692:       throw new Error(`Embedding dimension mismatch: expected ${state.dim}, got ${dim}`);
693:     }
694: 
695:     const rows = [];
696:     for (let index = 0; index < n; index += 1) {
697:       rows.push(Array.from(data.subarray(index * dim, (index + 1) * dim)));
698:     }
699:     return rows;
700:   }
701: 
702:   async function runExtractorBatch(extractor, texts) {
703:     if (texts.length === 0) {
704:       return [];
705:     }
706: 
707:     const t0 = performance.now();
708:     const run = Promise.resolve(extractorRunner(extractor)(texts, {
709:       pooling: 'mean',
710:       normalize: true,
711:     }));
712:     state.inFlightRawRuns.add(run);
713:     void run.finally(() => {
714:       state.inFlightRawRuns.delete(run);
715:     }).catch(() => undefined);
716: 
717:     const output = await withTimeout(
718:       run,
719:       state.inferenceTimeoutMs,
720:       `HF local inference timed out after ${state.inferenceTimeoutMs}ms`,
721:     );
722:     recordInferenceMs(performance.now() - t0);
723:     return sliceBatchOutput(output, texts.length);
724:   }
725: 
726:   async function getModel() {
727:     if (state.isDisposed) {
728:       throw new Error(`[hf-model-server] Server for ${state.modelName} has been disposed`);
729:     }
730:     if (state.extractor) {
731:       return state.extractor;
732:     }
733:     if (state.loadingPromise) {
734:       return state.loadingPromise;
735:     }
736: 
737:     state.serverState = 'loading';
738:     state.lastError = null;
739:     state.loadStartedAt = Date.now();
740:     state.loadProgressAt = state.loadStartedAt;
741:     state.loadingPromise = (async () => {
742:       const start = state.loadStartedAt;
743:       try {
744:         const result = await loadModel({
745:           modelName: state.modelName,
746:           dtype: state.dtype,
747:           modelLoadTimeoutMs: MODEL_LOAD_TIMEOUT,
748:           onLoadAttemptStart: (attempt = {}) => {
749:             const startedAt = Number.isFinite(attempt.startedAt) ? attempt.startedAt : Date.now();
750:             state.loadProgressAt = startedAt;
751:           },
752:         });
753:         const normalized = normalizeLoadResult(result, Date.now() - start);
754:         state.extractor = normalized.extractor;
755:         state.device = normalized.device;
756:         state.loadTimeMs = normalized.loadTimeMs;
757: 
758:         if (shouldSelfWarm) {
759:           try {
760:             await runExtractor(state.extractor, selfWarmInput);
761:           } catch (warmError) {
762:             // Self-warm is best-effort: the model loaded successfully, so a warm-up
763:             // failure must NOT pin the server to 'error' (probeModelServer
764:             // treats 'error' as dead and would reap a working server). Log + proceed.
765:             logger.warn?.(`[hf-model-server] self-warm failed (model still usable): ${getErrorMessage(warmError)}`);
766:           }
767:         }
768: 
769:         state.serverState = 'ready';
770:         return state.extractor;
771:       } catch (error) {
772:         state.loadingPromise = null;
773:         state.serverState = 'error';
774:         state.lastError = error;
775:         throw error;
776:       }
777:     })();
778: 
779:     return state.loadingPromise;
780:   }
781: 
782:   function healthPayload() {
783:     return {
784:       state: state.serverState,
785:       model: state.modelName,
786:       dim: state.dim > 0 ? state.dim : null,
787:       device: state.device,
788:       loadTimeMs: state.loadTimeMs,
789:       loadStartedAt: state.loadStartedAt,
790:       loadProgressAt: state.loadProgressAt,
791:       lastSuccessfulEmbedAt: state.lastSuccessfulEmbedAt,
792:       inFlight: state.inFlightRawRuns.size,
793:       queueDepth: state.inFlightRawRuns.size,
794:       timing: timingSummary(),
795:       error: state.lastError ? getErrorMessage(state.lastError) : undefined,
796:     };
797:   }
798: 
799:   async function embedPayload(body) {
800:     if (body === null || typeof body !== 'object' || Array.isArray(body)) {
801:       return {
802:         statusCode: 400,
803:         body: { error: 'POST /api/embed requires a JSON object body with an input array of strings' },
804:       };
805:     }
806:     const requestedModel = body.model || state.modelName;
807:     if (requestedModel !== state.modelName) {
808:       return {
809:         statusCode: 404,
810:         body: {
811:           error: `Model ${requestedModel} is not loaded by this hf-local server`,
812:           model: requestedModel,
813:           loadedModel: state.modelName,
814:         },
815:       };
816:     }
817:     if (!Array.isArray(body.input) || body.input.some((value) => typeof value !== 'string')) {
818:       return {
819:         statusCode: 400,
820:         body: { error: 'POST /api/embed requires input to be an array of strings' },
821:       };
822:     }
823: 
824:     try {
825:       const extractor = await getModel();
826:       let embeddings;
827:       if (state.supportsArrayInput !== false) {
828:         try {
829:           embeddings = await runExtractorBatch(extractor, body.input);
830:           if (body.input.length > 0) {
831:             state.supportsArrayInput = true;
832:             state.lastSuccessfulEmbedAt = Date.now();
833:           }
834:         } catch (error) {
835:           if (state.supportsArrayInput !== undefined || !isArrayInputUnsupported(error)) {
836:             throw error;
837:           }
838:           state.supportsArrayInput = false;
839:           logger.warn?.(`[hf-model-server] extractor rejected batched array input; falling back to per-text inference for this server: ${getErrorMessage(error)}`);
840:         }
841:       }
842: 
843:       if (!embeddings) {
844:         embeddings = [];
845:         for (const text of body.input) {
846:           const embedding = await runExtractor(extractor, text);
847:           state.lastSuccessfulEmbedAt = Date.now();
848:           embeddings.push(embedding);
849:         }
850:       }
851:       return {
852:         statusCode: 200,
853:         body: {
854:           embeddings,
855:           dim: state.dim,
856:         },
857:       };
858:     } catch (error) {
859:       logger.warn?.(`[hf-model-server] Embed failed: ${getErrorMessage(error)}`);
860:       return {
861:         statusCode: 500,
862:         body: { error: getErrorMessage(error) },
863:       };
864:     }
865:   }
866: 
867:   async function routeJson(method, routePath, body = {}) {
868:     if (method === 'GET' && routePath === HEALTH_PATH) {
869:       return {
870:         statusCode: 200,
871:         body: healthPayload(),
872:       };
873:     }
874:     if (method === 'POST' && routePath === EMBED_PATH) {
875:       return await embedPayload(body);
876:     }
877:     return {
878:       statusCode: 404,
879:       body: { error: `Unknown route: ${method || 'GET'} ${routePath}` },
880:     };
881:   }
882: 
883:   function writeJson(response, statusCode, payload) {
884:     const body = JSON.stringify(payload);
885:     response.writeHead(statusCode, {
886:       'content-type': 'application/json',
887:       'content-length': Buffer.byteLength(body),
888:     });
889:     response.end(body);
890:   }
891: 
892:   async function readJsonBody(request) {
893:     return await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
894:       let body = '';
895:       request.setEncoding('utf8');
896:       request.on('data', (chunk) => {
897:         body += chunk;
898:         if (Buffer.byteLength(body, 'utf8') > MAX_REQUEST_BYTES) {
899:           reject(new Error(`Request body exceeds ${MAX_REQUEST_BYTES} bytes`));
900:           request.destroy();
901:         }
902:       });
903:       request.on('end', () => {
904:         try {
905:           resolve(body.trim() ? JSON.parse(body) : {});
906:         } catch (error) {
907:           reject(new Error(`Invalid JSON body: ${getErrorMessage(error)}`));
908:         }
909:       });
910:       request.on('error', reject);
911:     });
912:   }
913: 
914:   async function requestHandler(request, response) {
915:     const url = new URL(request.url || '/', 'http://127.0.0.1');
916:     let body = {};
917:     if (request.method === 'POST') {
918:       try {
919:         body = await readJsonBody(request);
920:       } catch (error) {
921:         writeJson(response, 400, { error: getErrorMessage(error) });
922:         return;
923:       }
924:     }
925:     const result = await routeJson(request.method || 'GET', url.pathname, body);
926:     writeJson(response, result.statusCode, result.body);
927:   }
928: 
929:   const server = http.createServer((request, response) => {
930:     void requestHandler(request, response).catch((error) => {
931:       writeJson(response, 500, { error: getErrorMessage(error) });
932:     });
933:   });
934: 
935:   server.on('connection', (socket) => {
936:     activeSockets.add(socket);
937:     socket.once('close', () => {
938:       activeSockets.delete(socket);
939:     });
940:   });
941: 
942:   async function listen(target = resolveListenTarget(options)) {
943:     state.listenTarget = target;
944:     const endpoint = await listenServer(server, target, listenGuardOptions);
945:     state.listenedEndpoint = endpoint;
946:     void getModel().catch((error) => {
947:       logger.error?.(`[hf-model-server] Model load failed: ${getErrorMessage(error)}`);
948:     });
949:     return {
950:       server,
951:       socketPath: endpoint,
952:       endpoint,
953:       close,
954:       dispose,
955:       inject,
956:       getState: () => healthPayload(),
957:     };
958:   }
959: 
960:   async function close(closeOptions = {}) {
961:     const disposeModel = closeOptions.disposeModel === true;
962:     for (const socket of activeSockets) {
963:       socket.destroy();
964:     }
965:     activeSockets.clear();
966: 
967:     await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
968:       if (!server.listening) {
969:         resolve();
970:         return;
971:       }
972:       server.close((error) => {
973:         if (error) {
974:           reject(error);
975:           return;
976:         }
977:         resolve();
978:       });
979:     });
980: 
981:     if (state.listenTarget && !state.listenTarget.startsWith('tcp://')) {
982:       try {
983:         fs.unlinkSync(state.listenTarget);
984:       } catch (error) {
985:         if (error.code !== 'ENOENT') {
986:           throw error;
987:         }
988:       }
989:     }
990: 
991:     if (disposeModel) {
992:       await dispose();
993:     }
994:   }
995: 
996:   async function dispose() {
997:     if (state.disposePromise) {
998:       return state.disposePromise;
999:     }
1000: 
1001:     state.isDisposed = true;
1002:     state.disposePromise = (async () => {
1003:       if (state.inFlightRawRuns.size > 0) {
1004:         await withTimeout(
1005:           Promise.allSettled(Array.from(state.inFlightRawRuns)),
1006:           INFERENCE_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS,
1007:           `[hf-model-server] Timed out waiting for native inference drain after ${INFERENCE_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS}ms`,
1008:         );
1009:       }
1010: 
1011:       const loading = state.loadingPromise;
1012:       if (loading) {
1013:         await withTimeout(
1014:           loading.then(() => undefined, () => undefined),
1015:           MODEL_LOAD_TIMEOUT,
1016:           `[hf-model-server] Timed out waiting for model load before dispose after ${MODEL_LOAD_TIMEOUT}ms`,
1017:         );
1018:       }
1019: 
1020:       const extractor = state.extractor;
1021:       state.extractor = null;
1022:       state.loadingPromise = null;
1023:       state.loadTimeMs = null;
1024:       state.loadStartedAt = null;
1025:       state.loadProgressAt = null;
1026:       state.serverState = 'loading';
1027:       if (!extractor) {
1028:         return;
1029:       }
1030: 
1031:       assertSingleSessionBeforeDispose(extractor);
1032:       await extractor.dispose();
1033:     })();
1034: 
1035:     return state.disposePromise;
1036:   }
1037: 
1038:   async function inject(method, routePath, body = {}) {
1039:     return await routeJson(method, routePath, body);
1040:   }
1041: 
1042:   return {
1043:     server,
1044:     listen,
1045:     close,
1046:     dispose,
1047:     getModel,
1048:     inject,
1049:     getState: () => healthPayload(),
1050:   };
1051: }
1052: 
1053: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1054: // 6. CLI ENTRYPOINT
1055: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1056: 
1057: async function main() {
1058:   const app = createHfModelServer();
1059:   const handle = await app.listen();
1060:   process.stderr.write(`[hf-model-server] listening at ${handle.endpoint}\n`);
1061: 
1062:   const shutdown = (signal) => {
1063:     process.stderr.write(`[hf-model-server] received ${signal}; shutting down\n`);
1064:     app.close({ disposeModel: true })
1065:       .then(() => process.exit(0))
1066:       .catch((error) => {
1067:         process.stderr.write(`[hf-model-server] shutdown failed: ${getErrorMessage(error)}\n`);
1068:         process.exit(1);
1069:       });
1070:   };
1071: 
1072:   for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM', 'SIGHUP', 'SIGQUIT']) {
1073:     process.once(signal, () => shutdown(signal));
1074:   }
1075: }
1076: 
1077: module.exports = {
1078:   ALLOWED_HF_LOCAL_DTYPES,
1079:   DEFAULT_MODEL,
1080:   INFERENCE_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS,
1081:   LOOPBACK_BIND_HOSTS,
1082:   MODEL_LOAD_TIMEOUT,
1083:   SOCKET_FILE_NAME,
1084:   SOCKET_RESIDENT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS,
1085:   assertLoopbackBindAllowed,
1086:   assertSingleSessionBeforeDispose,
1087:   assertSocketDirOwnership,
1088:   createHfModelServer,
1089:   defaultDbDir,
1090:   getOptimalDevice,
1091:   getSessionCount,
1092:   importTransformers,
1093:   isLoopbackHost,
1094:   listenHttpServer,
1095:   loadHfModel,
1096:   probeSocketResident,
1097:   resolveDtype,
1098:   resolveListenTarget,
1099:   toConnectionOptions,
1100: };
1101: 
1102: if (require.main === module) {
1103:   main().catch((error) => {
1104:     process.stderr.write(`[hf-model-server] ${error.stack || getErrorMessage(error)}\n`);
1105:     process.exit(1);
1106:   });
1107: }

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // [mk-code-index-launcher] MCP child-process launcher for the mk-code-index server
3: // (system-code-graph). Loads project-local env overrides, applies the maintainer-mode
4: // INDEX_* override when SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true, auto-migrates the
5: // code-graph database from the former shared location (.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database/)
6: // back to the skill-local location (mcp_server/database/), ensures dist
7: // artifacts are built and current, serializes concurrent starts via a filesystem bootstrap
8: // lock, sets SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR for the child process (operator override wins),
9: // then spawns the code-graph MCP server child. All stderr lines are tagged with the
10: // bracketed module prefix for ops grepping. See .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/ for
11: // the standalone skill that owns the server source.
12:

15: const fs = require('fs');

23: const DOTENV_ALLOW_RE = /^(SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_|SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN$|MK_CODE_INDEX_)/;

25: // Load project-local env overrides BEFORE spawning the MCP child. .env.local wins over
26: // .env, both are gitignored. Existing process.env wins over file values (do not override).
27: // Minimal parser — no external dependency.
28: function loadEnvFile(filePath) {
29:   if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return 0;
30:   let count = 0;
31:   const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
32:   for (const line of raw.split('\n')) {
33:     const trimmed = line.trim();
34:     if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('#')) continue;
35:     const m = trimmed.match(/^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.*)$/);
36:     if (!m) continue;
37:     const key = m[1];
38:     let val = m[2];
39:     // Strip matching outer quotes (single or double)
40:     if ((val.startsWith('"') && val.endsWith('"')) || (val.startsWith("'") && val.endsWith("'"))) {
41:       val = val.slice(1, -1);
42:     }
43:     // The minimal parser is line-oriented; embedded \n in a quoted value would
44:     // already have terminated the line, but defend explicitly.
45:     if (val.includes('\n') || val.includes('\0')) {
46:       process.stderr.write(`[mk-code-index-launcher] env value for ${key} contains control chars; skipping\n`);
47:       continue;
48:     }
49:     if (!DOTENV_ALLOW_RE.test(key)) {
50:       process.stderr.write(`[mk-code-index-launcher] env ${key} from ${path.basename(filePath)} is not allowlisted; skipping\n`);
51:       continue;
52:     }
53:     if (!(key in process.env)) {
54:       process.env[key] = val;
55:       count++;
56:     }
57:   }
58:   return count;
59: }

64:   return v === '0' || v === 'false' || v === 'no' || v === 'off' || v === '';

67: // Category -> INDEX_* env key. Module-level so the launcher and its tests share one source.
68: const MAINTAINER_CATEGORY_ENV = {
69:   skills: 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS',
70:   agents: 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS',
71:   commands: 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS',
72:   specs: 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS',

77: // "false"/empty/unset forces none, and a comma list forces the recognized subset (unknown names
78: // dropped). Returns category names; the caller maps them to env keys via MAINTAINER_CATEGORY_ENV.
79: function resolveMaintainerModeCategories(rawValue) {

96:   // categories to force-index on this machine, overriding whatever the runtime's MCP config
97:   // injected. Committed configs ship "false" (end-user safe: only code outside .opencode is
98:   // graphed); a maintainer opts in locally via .env.local (gitignored). Accepts "true" (all

101:   // Per-call code_graph_scan args (includeSkills, etc.) still override env. See ENV_REFERENCE.md § GRAPH.

204: function canonicalizePath(pathValue) {

206:   try {
207:     return fs.realpathSync.native(resolvedPath);

214: function canonicalizeExistingPrefix(pathValue) {
215:   const resolvedPath = path.resolve(pathValue);
216:   if (fs.existsSync(resolvedPath)) return fs.realpathSync.native(resolvedPath);
217: 
218:   const segments = [];
219:   let current = resolvedPath;

223:     segments.unshift(path.basename(current));

275: function legacyLeasePaths() {
276:   // After the 2026-05-29 relocation, skill-local (mcp_server/database/) is the PRIMARY path.
277:   // The legacy probe now covers the former shared `.spec-kit` location plus the pre-rename
278:   // `skill/` (singular) typo dir, so a launcher still holding an old-location lease is detected.
279:   return [
280:     path.join(opencodeDir, '.spec-kit', 'code-graph', 'database', PID_FILE_NAME),
281:     path.join(opencodeDir, 'skill', 'system-code-graph', 'mcp_server', 'database', PID_FILE_NAME),
282:   ].map(canonicalizePath);

285: function readLeaseFile(filePath = leasePath()) {
286:   try {
287:     const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
288:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
289:     if (typeof parsed.pid === 'number') return parsed;
290:   } catch {
291:     // Missing or corrupt lease files are treated as no active lease.
292:   }
293:   return null;
294: }
295: 
296: function readOwnerLeaseFile(filePath = ownerLeasePath()) {
297:   try {
298:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
299:     if (
300:       Number.isInteger(parsed.ownerPid) &&
301:       Number.isInteger(parsed.ppid) &&
302:       typeof parsed.executablePath === 'string' &&
303:       typeof parsed.startedAtIso === 'string' &&
304:       typeof parsed.lastHeartbeatIso === 'string' &&
305:       Number.isInteger(parsed.ttlMs) &&
306:       typeof parsed.canonicalDbDir === 'string'
307:     ) {
308:       return parsed;
309:     }
310:   } catch {
311:     // Missing or corrupt owner leases are treated as no active owner.
312:   }
313:   return null;
314: }
315: 
316: function writeOwnerLeaseFile(lease) {
317:   const currentLeasePath = path.join(ensureCanonicalDir(path.dirname(ownerLeasePath())), OWNER_LEASE_FILE_NAME);
318:   const tmp = `${currentLeasePath}.tmp.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}.${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
319:   fs.writeFileSync(tmp, `${JSON.stringify(lease, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600, flag: 'wx' });
320:   fs.renameSync(tmp, currentLeasePath);
321: }
322: 
323: function writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive(lease) {
324:   const currentLeasePath = path.join(ensureCanonicalDir(path.dirname(ownerLeasePath())), OWNER_LEASE_FILE_NAME);
325:   let fd;
326:   try {
327:     fd = fs.openSync(currentLeasePath, 'wx', 0o600);
328:     fs.writeFileSync(fd, `${JSON.stringify(lease, null, 2)}\n`, 'utf8');
329:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
330:     return true;
331:   } catch (error) {
332:     if (error.code === 'EEXIST') return false;
333:     throw error;
334:   } finally {
335:     if (typeof fd === 'number') fs.closeSync(fd);
336:   }
337: }

339: // The socket path this owner's environment computes. Recording it in the
340: // leases lets a secondary under a divergent SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR bridge
341: // to the REAL socket instead of probing a recomputed wrong one and
342: // misreporting a healthy owner as no-bridge-socket.
343: function ownerSocketPath() {
344:   try {
345:     const { getIpcSocketPath } = loadBridgeModule();
346:     if (typeof getIpcSocketPath !== 'function') return null;
347:     return getIpcSocketPath('mk-code-index', { dbDir: resolvedDbDir() }) ?? null;
348:   } catch {
349:     return null;
350:   }
351: }
352: 
353: let ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer = null;
354: 
355: function clearOwnerLeaseHeartbeat() {
356:   if (ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer) {
357:     clearInterval(ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer);
358:     ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer = null;
359:   }
360: }
361: 
362: // Without a periodic refresh, every healthy long-running owner classifies
363: // as stale-heartbeat-reclaim about two minutes after spawn, so each later
364: // secondary start rewrites the owner lease before rediscovering the live
365: // PID lease — churn plus reclaim-race exposure for nothing.
366: function startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat(ownerPid) {
367:   clearOwnerLeaseHeartbeat();
368:   const lease = readOwnerLeaseFile();
369:   const ttlMs = Number.isFinite(lease?.ttlMs) && lease.ttlMs > 0 ? lease.ttlMs : 60000;
370:   const intervalMs = Math.max(1000, Math.floor(ttlMs / 2));
371:   ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer = setInterval(() => {
372:     if (refreshOwnerLeaseFile(ownerPid)) return;
373:     // A failed refresh means a concurrent reclaim superseded this owner;
374:     // this launcher exits with its child rather than supervising relaunches,
375:     // so stop heartbeating instead of fighting the new owner.
376:     log('owner lease heartbeat refresh failed (superseded); stopping heartbeat');
377:     clearOwnerLeaseHeartbeat();
378:   }, intervalMs);
379:   ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer.unref?.();
380: }

382: function buildOwnerLease(ownerPid = process.pid) {
383:   return {
384:     ownerPid,
385:     ppid: process.ppid,
386:     executablePath: process.execPath,
387:     startedAtIso: new Date().toISOString(),
388:     lastHeartbeatIso: new Date().toISOString(),
389:     ttlMs: 60000,
390:     canonicalDbDir: resolvedDbDir(),
391:     socketPath: ownerSocketPath(),
392:   };
393: }

395: function processLiveness(pid) {
396:   if (!Number.isInteger(pid) || pid <= 0) return 'dead';
397:   try {
398:     process.kill(pid, 0);
399:     return 'alive';
400:   } catch (error) {
401:     if (error.code === 'ESRCH') return 'dead';
402:     if (error.code === 'EPERM') return 'unknown-eperm';
403:     return 'alive';
404:   }
405: }
406: 
407: function readParentPid(pid) {
408:   if (!Number.isInteger(pid) || pid <= 0) return null;
409:   if (process.platform === 'linux') {
410:     try {
411:       const status = fs.readFileSync(`/proc/${pid}/status`, 'utf8');
412:       const match = status.match(/^PPid:\s+(\d+)$/m);
413:       return match ? Number.parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;
414:     } catch {
415:       return null;
416:     }
417:   }
418:   const result = spawnSync('ps', ['-o', 'ppid=', '-p', String(pid)], {
419:     encoding: 'utf8',
420:     stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'ignore'],
421:   });
422:   if (result.status !== 0 || !result.stdout) return null;
423:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(result.stdout.trim(), 10);
424:   return Number.isInteger(parsed) ? parsed : null;
425: }
426: 
427: function classifyOwnerLease(lease) {
428:   const liveness = processLiveness(lease.ownerPid);
429:   if (liveness === 'dead') return 'stale-pid';
430:   if (liveness === 'unknown-eperm') return 'unknown-eperm';
431: 
432:   const actualPpid = readParentPid(lease.ownerPid);
433:   if (actualPpid !== null && actualPpid !== lease.ppid && actualPpid === 1) {
434:     return 'ppid-1-orphan';
435:   }
436: 
437:   const heartbeatMs = Date.parse(lease.lastHeartbeatIso);
438:   const ttlMs = Number.isFinite(lease.ttlMs) ? lease.ttlMs : 0;
439:   if (!Number.isFinite(heartbeatMs) || Date.now() - heartbeatMs > ttlMs * 2) {
440:     return 'stale-heartbeat-reclaim';
441:   }
442: 
443:   return 'live-owner';
444: }

446: function acquireOwnerLeaseFile() {
447:   const currentOwnerLeasePath = ownerLeasePath();
448:   const existing = readOwnerLeaseFile(currentOwnerLeasePath);
449: 
450:   if (existing) {
451:     const classification = classifyOwnerLease(existing);
452:     if (classification === 'live-owner' || classification === 'unknown-eperm') {
453:       return { acquired: false, holder: existing, classification };
454:     }
455:     log(`ownerLeaseReclaimed: ${classification} ownerPid=${existing.ownerPid}`);
456:   }
457: 
458:   const lease = buildOwnerLease(process.pid);
459:   // Reclaim a stale lease by removing it first, then claim exclusively (O_EXCL).
460:   // This collapses the fresh-acquire and stale-reclaim paths into a single CAS so
461:   // only one racer can win: the loser's exclusive create hits EEXIST and returns
462:   // acquired:false instead of last-writer-wins overwriting the winner's lease.
463:   if (existing) {
464:     try {
465:       fs.unlinkSync(currentOwnerLeasePath);
466:     } catch (error) {
467:       if (error.code !== 'ENOENT') throw error;
468:     }
469:   }
470:   if (!writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive(lease)) {
471:     const holder = readOwnerLeaseFile(currentOwnerLeasePath);
472:     return {
473:       acquired: false,
474:       holder: holder || lease,
475:       classification: holder ? classifyOwnerLease(holder) : 'live-owner',
476:     };
477:   }
478:   ownerLeasePid = process.pid;
479:   return { acquired: true, lease, reclaimed: existing };
480: }

482: function refreshOwnerLeaseFile(ownerPid, patch = {}) {
483:   const lease = readOwnerLeaseFile();
484:   if (!lease || lease.ownerPid !== ownerPid) return false;
485:   const nextOwnerPid = patch.ownerPid ?? ownerPid;
486:   writeOwnerLeaseFile({
487:     ...lease,
488:     ...patch,
489:     lastHeartbeatIso: new Date().toISOString(),
490:   });
491:   // Re-read after the atomic write; if a concurrent reclaim superseded us between
492:   // the ownership check and the write, do not claim the refresh succeeded.
493:   const reread = readOwnerLeaseFile();
494:   if (!reread || reread.ownerPid !== nextOwnerPid) return false;
495:   ownerLeasePid = nextOwnerPid;
496:   return true;
497: }
498: 
499: function clearOwnerLeaseFile() {
500:   if (!Number.isInteger(ownerLeasePid)) return;
501:   try {
502:     // Re-confirm ownership immediately before unlink so a successor lease written
503:     // after the first read is not deleted by path. A residual sub-syscall window remains; fully
504:     // closing it would require the launcher to share owner-lease.ts's mutation lock.
505:     const lease = readOwnerLeaseFile();
506:     if (lease && lease.ownerPid === ownerLeasePid
507:         && readOwnerLeaseFile()?.ownerPid === ownerLeasePid) {
508:       fs.unlinkSync(ownerLeasePath());
509:     }
510:   } catch {
511:     // Idempotent cleanup.
512:   } finally {
513:     ownerLeasePid = null;
514:   }
515: }
516: 
517: function clearOwnerLeaseFileIfOwner(ownerPid) {
518:   try {
519:     // Re-confirm ownership immediately before unlink (see clearOwnerLeaseFile).
520:     const lease = readOwnerLeaseFile();
521:     if (lease && lease.ownerPid === ownerPid
522:         && readOwnerLeaseFile()?.ownerPid === ownerPid) {
523:       fs.unlinkSync(ownerLeasePath());
524:     }
525:   } catch {
526:     // Idempotent cleanup.
527:   }
528: }

530: function pidLiveAt(filePath, pidField) {
531:   try {
532:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
533:     const pid = parsed[pidField];
534:     if (!Number.isInteger(pid)) return false;
535:     try { process.kill(pid, 0); return true; } // live
536:     catch (error) { return error.code === 'EPERM'; } // EPERM = exists but no perm → treat live; ESRCH = dead
537:   } catch { return false; } // missing/unreadable → not live
538: }
539: 
540: // A former-location DB is still "owned" if EITHER the launcher PID lease
541: // (.mk-code-index-launcher.json) or the owner lease (.code-graph-owner.json) names a live process.
542: function formerLocationOwnerLive(dir) {
543:   return pidLiveAt(path.join(dir, '.mk-code-index-launcher.json'), 'pid')
544:       || pidLiveAt(path.join(dir, '.code-graph-owner.json'), 'ownerPid');
545: }

547: function leaseHeldFromFile(filePath, legacyPath = null) {
548:   const lease = readLeaseFile(filePath);
549:   if (!lease) return { held: false, ownerPid: null, staleReclaimable: false, startedAt: null, legacyPath, socketPath: null };
550:   // Surface the owner-recorded socket path so the bridge prefers it over
551:   // recomputing one that may diverge under a different SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR.
552:   const socketPath = typeof lease.socketPath === 'string' && lease.socketPath.length > 0 ? lease.socketPath : null;
553:   // Do not bridge to a LEGACY-location lease unless its lease file is owned by the
554:   // current user. A foreign-owned lease in a shared/former path could otherwise point this
555:   // client at a spoofed IPC socket.
556:   if (legacyPath && typeof process.getuid === 'function') {
557:     try {
558:       if (fs.statSync(filePath).uid !== process.getuid()) {
559:         return { held: false, ownerPid: null, staleReclaimable: false, startedAt: null, legacyPath, socketPath: null };
560:       }
561:     } catch {
562:       return { held: false, ownerPid: null, staleReclaimable: false, startedAt: null, legacyPath, socketPath: null };
563:     }
564:   }
565:   const startedAt = lease.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
566:   try {
567:     process.kill(lease.pid, 0);
568:     return { held: true, ownerPid: lease.pid, staleReclaimable: false, startedAt, legacyPath, socketPath };
569:   } catch (error) {
570:     if (error.code === 'ESRCH') return { held: false, ownerPid: lease.pid, staleReclaimable: true, startedAt, legacyPath, socketPath };
571:     // Mirror skill-advisor parity — EPERM means the process exists but we lack permission (e.g. sandbox); treat as live lease.
572:     if (error.code === 'EPERM') return { held: true, ownerPid: lease.pid, staleReclaimable: false, startedAt, legacyPath, socketPath };
573:     throw error;
574:   }
575: }
576: 
577: function isLeaseHeld() {
578:   const primary = leaseHeldFromFile(leasePath());
579:   if (primary.held) return primary;
580: 
581:   for (const legacyPath of legacyLeasePaths()) {
582:     if (legacyPath === leasePath()) continue;
583:     const legacy = leaseHeldFromFile(legacyPath, legacyPath);
584:     if (legacy.held || legacy.staleReclaimable) return legacy;
585:   }
586: 
587:   return primary;
588: }

590: function writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, suffix = '') {
591:   const startedAt = leaseResult.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
592:   const legacyMarker = leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : '';
593:   process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${leaseResult.ownerPid} startedAt=${startedAt}${legacyMarker}${suffix}\n`);
594: }

596: async function waitForPidExit(pid, timeoutMs) {
597:   const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
598:   while (Date.now() <= deadline) {
599:     if (processLiveness(pid) === 'dead') return true;
600:     await sleep(100);
601:   }
602:   return processLiveness(pid) === 'dead';
603: }
604: 
605: async function reapOwnerBeforeRespawn(ownerPid) {
606:   const liveness = processLiveness(ownerPid);
607:   if (liveness === 'unknown-eperm') {
608:     return { allowed: false, reason: 'owner-liveness-unknown-eperm' };
609:   }
610:   if (liveness === 'dead') {
611:     return { allowed: true, reason: 'owner-already-dead' };
612:   }
613: 
614:   log(`confirmed-dead socket; reaping recorded code-index owner pid ${ownerPid} before respawn`);
615:   try {
616:     process.kill(ownerPid, 'SIGTERM');
617:   } catch (error) {
618:     if (error.code !== 'ESRCH') throw error;
619:   }
620:   const exitedAfterTerm = await waitForPidExit(ownerPid, RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS);
621:   if (!exitedAfterTerm) {
622:     log(`code-index owner pid ${ownerPid} exceeded ${RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS}ms reap grace; sending SIGKILL`);
623:     try {
624:       process.kill(ownerPid, 'SIGKILL');
625:     } catch (error) {
626:       if (error.code !== 'ESRCH') throw error;
627:     }
628:     await waitForPidExit(ownerPid, 1000);
629:   }
630:   return { allowed: true, reason: 'owner-reaped' };
631: }

633: async function respawnAfterDeadSocket(leaseResult, decision, options = {}) {
634:   if (process.env.SPECKIT_BRIDGE_RESPAWN_DISABLED === '1') {
635:     writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ' (dead-socket-respawn-disabled)');
636:     return { action: 'report', reason: 'respawn-disabled', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
637:   }
638: 
639:   const ownerPid = options.respawnChildPid;
640:   if (!Number.isInteger(ownerPid) || ownerPid <= 0) {
641:     log('confirmed-dead socket but no recorded code-index child owner pid is available; respawn inert');
642:     writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ' (dead-socket-no-child-pid)');
643:     return { action: 'report', reason: 'missing-child-pid', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
644:   }
645: 
646:   let bootstrapLockHeld = false;
647:   try {
648:     bootstrapLockHeld = await acquireBootstrapLock({ requireLock: true });
649:     const currentOwner = readOwnerLeaseFile();
650:     if (currentOwner?.ownerPid !== ownerPid) {
651:       log('dead-socket respawn skipped; code-index owner lease changed while waiting for respawn lock');
652:       writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ' (dead-socket-respawn-superseded)');
653:       return { action: 'report', reason: 'respawn-superseded', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
654:     }
655: 
656:     const reapResult = await reapOwnerBeforeRespawn(ownerPid);
657:     if (!reapResult.allowed) {
658:       log(`dead-socket respawn skipped; ${reapResult.reason} for ownerPid=${ownerPid}`);
659:       writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ` (dead-socket-${reapResult.reason})`);
660:       return { action: 'report', reason: reapResult.reason, socketPath: decision.socketPath };
661:     }
662: 
663:     clearOwnerLeaseFileIfOwner(ownerPid);
664:     const lease = buildOwnerLease(process.pid);
665:     if (!writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive(lease)) {
666:       const holder = readOwnerLeaseFile();
667:       log(`dead-socket respawn skipped; another launcher owns code-index owner lease pid=${holder?.ownerPid ?? 'unknown'}`);
668:       writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ' (dead-socket-respawn-owned)');
669:       return { action: 'report', reason: 'respawn-lock-held', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
670:     }
671:     ownerLeasePid = process.pid;
672: 
673:     buildIfNeeded([]);
674:     writeLeaseFile();
675:     launchServer();
676:     return { action: 'respawn', reason: 'spawned', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
677:   } finally {
678:     if (bootstrapLockHeld) {
679:       fs.rmSync(lockDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
680:     }
681:   }
682: }

684: async function bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld(leaseResult, options = {}) {
685:   const { maybeBridgeLeaseHolder } = loadBridgeModule();
686:   const decision = await maybeBridgeLeaseHolder({
687:     serviceName: 'mk-code-index',
688:     leaseResult,
689:     loggerPrefix: 'mk-code-index-launcher',
690:     dbDir: resolvedDbDir(),
691:     bridge: bridgeStdioThroughSessionProxy,
692:   });
693:   if (decision && decision.action === 'respawn') {
694:     return await respawnAfterDeadSocket(leaseResult, decision, options);
695:   }
696:   return decision;
697: }

721: function run(command, args, options = {}) {
722:   const result = spawnSync(command, args, {
723:     cwd: options.cwd || root,
724:     env: buildChildEnv(),
725:     encoding: 'utf8',
726:     stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
727:   });
728: 
729:   if (result.stdout) {
730:     process.stderr.write(result.stdout);
731:   }
732:   if (result.stderr) {
733:     process.stderr.write(result.stderr);
734:   }
735:   if (result.error) {
736:     throw result.error;
737:   }
738:   if (result.status !== 0) {
739:     throw new Error(`${command} ${args.join(' ')} exited ${result.status}`);
740:   }
741: }

743: function buildChildEnv(extra = {}) {
744:   const nextEnv = {};
745:   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(process.env)) {
746:     if (BLOCKED_CHILD_ENV_RE.test(key)) continue;
747:     nextEnv[key] = value;
748:   }
749:   return { ...nextEnv, ...extra };
750: }

752: function ensureLayout(actions) {

754:     fs.renameSync(legacySkillDir, skillsDir);

756:     actions.push('promoted .opencode/skill to .opencode/skills and created compatibility symlink');
757:     refreshPaths();
758:     return;

761:   if (exists(skillsDir) && exists(legacySkillDir) && !fs.lstatSync(legacySkillDir).isSymbolicLink()) {
762:     const backup = path.join(opencodeDir, `skill_legacy_backup_${new Date().toISOString().replace(/[-:]/g, '').replace(/\..+/, 'Z')}`);
763:     fs.renameSync(legacySkillDir, backup);
764:     fs.symlinkSync('skills', legacySkillDir, 'dir');
765:     actions.push(`moved legacy .opencode/skill to ${rel(backup)} and created compatibility symlink`);
766:     refreshPaths();
767:     return;

771:   // up consumers of the singular path, so the bridge no longer needs to be
772:   // recreated on every MCP startup. Migration paths above (rename / move-aside)
773:   // still create the symlink when an actual legacy singular dir is present.

812: const BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_STALE_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 minutes — fallback for unstamped (legacy) lock dirs
813: const BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = 120000;

816: // Returns the pid recorded inside the lock dir, or null when no readable pid
817: // stamp exists (legacy lock dirs, or a holder that died before stamping).
818: function readBootstrapLockOwnerPid() {
819:   try {
820:     const raw = fs.readFileSync(path.join(lockDir, BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_OWNER_FILE), 'utf8').trim();
821:     const pid = Number.parseInt(raw, 10);
822:     return Number.isInteger(pid) && pid > 0 ? pid : null;
823:   } catch {
824:     return null;
825:   }
826: }
827: 
828: // Reclaim a stale bootstrap lockdir. A lock is reclaimable as soon as its
829: // recorded holder is provably dead; the mtime TTL is only a fallback for lock
830: // dirs with no readable pid stamp (legacy dirs) or a holder whose liveness
831: // cannot be determined. Without the dead-holder check, a holder killed less than
832: // staleMs ago wedges every requireLock respawn for the full wait deadline.
833: function removeStaleBootstrapLock(staleMs = BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_STALE_MS) {
834:   let ageMs;
835:   try {
836:     ageMs = Date.now() - fs.statSync(lockDir).mtimeMs;
837:   } catch (error) {
838:     if (error.code === 'ENOENT') return false;
839:     throw error;
840:   }
841:   const ownerPid = readBootstrapLockOwnerPid();
842:   const ownerDead = ownerPid !== null && processLiveness(ownerPid) === 'dead';
843:   if (!ownerDead && ageMs <= staleMs) {
844:     return false;
845:   }
846:   // Atomically CLAIM the stale lockdir via rename before deleting it. Only one
847:   // racer wins the rename; a successor that mkdir's a fresh lockDir after our stat
848:   // creates a NEW inode that our rename/rmSync cannot touch, so we never delete a
849:   // live successor lock. A losing racer's rename throws ENOENT and falls through to
850:   // the outer retry.
851:   const staleClaim = `${lockDir}.stale.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}`;
852:   try {
853:     fs.renameSync(lockDir, staleClaim);
854:     fs.rmSync(staleClaim, { recursive: true, force: true });
855:     const reason = ownerDead ? `dead holder pid=${ownerPid}` : `mtime ${Math.round(ageMs / 1000)}s old`;
856:     log(`reclaiming stale bootstrap lock ${rel(lockDir)} (${reason})`);
857:     return true;
858:   } catch (error) {
859:     if (error.code === 'ENOENT' || error.code === 'ENOTEMPTY') return false;
860:     throw error;
861:   }
862: }
863: 
864: async function acquireBootstrapLock(options = {}) {
865:   const requireLock = options.requireLock === true;
866:   fs.mkdirSync(dbDir, { recursive: true });
867:   const deadline = Date.now() + (options.timeoutMs ?? BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS);
868:   const staleMs = options.staleMs ?? BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_STALE_MS;
869:   const retrySleepMs = options.retrySleepMs ?? 1000;
870:   while (true) {
871:     try {
872:       fs.mkdirSync(lockDir);
873:       // Stamp the holder pid so a later launcher can reclaim this lock the
874:       // instant we die, instead of waiting out the mtime TTL. Best-effort: a
875:       // failed stamp degrades to the TTL path, never blocks acquisition.
876:       try {
877:         fs.writeFileSync(path.join(lockDir, BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_OWNER_FILE), String(process.pid), { mode: 0o600 });
878:       } catch { /* TTL fallback covers an unstamped lock */ }
879:       return true;
880:     } catch (error) {
881:       if (error.code !== 'EEXIST') {
882:         throw error;
883:       }
884:       if (artifactsReady() && !requireLock) {
885:         return false;
886:       }
887:       if (removeStaleBootstrapLock(staleMs)) {
888:         continue;
889:       }
890:       if (Date.now() > deadline) {
891:         throw new Error(`bootstrap lock timed out at ${rel(lockDir)}`);
892:       }
893:       await sleep(retrySleepMs);
894:     }
895:   }
896: }
897: 
898: function launchServer() {
899:   if (launchStarted) {
900:     log('launchServer skipped: launch already started in this launcher process');
901:     return false;
902:   }
903:   launchStarted = true;
904:   // Set DB dir for the child process (operator-set env var wins).
905:   if (!process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR) {
906:     process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR = resolvedDbDir();
907:   }
908: 
909:   const server = path.join(kitDir, 'mcp_server', 'dist', 'index.js');
910:   childProcess = spawn(process.execPath, [server], {
911:     cwd: root,
912:     env: buildChildEnv(),
913:     stdio: 'inherit',
914:   });
915: 
916:   if (typeof childProcess.pid === 'number') {
917:     const refreshed = refreshOwnerLeaseFile(process.pid, {
918:       ownerPid: childProcess.pid,
919:       ppid: process.pid,
920:       executablePath: process.execPath,
921:     });
922:     if (!refreshed) {
923:       log('owner lease refresh to child pid failed; launcher pid remains the recorded owner');
924:     }
925:     startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat(refreshed ? childProcess.pid : process.pid);
926:   }
927: 
928:   childProcess.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
929:     if (signal) {
930:       // Clear the lease before mirroring the signal; process.on('exit') does not fire on SIGKILL.
931:       clearAllLeaseFiles();
932:       // Remove our own handler for this signal first. Without this, the self-sent
933:       // catchable signal (SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP/SIGQUIT) re-enters installSignalHandlers'
934:       // handler, which — for an externally-killed child (childProcess.killed === false) —
935:       // exits 0, so a supervising runtime misreads abnormal daemon death as a clean
936:       // shutdown. Removing the handler lets the re-raised signal terminate this process
937:       // with the signaled status (128 + n). SIGKILL is uncatchable and already mirrors.
938:       process.removeAllListeners(signal);
939:       process.kill(process.pid, signal);
940:       return;
941:     }
942:     clearAllLeaseFiles();
943:     process.exit(code ?? 0);
944:   });
945: 
946:   childProcess.on('error', (error) => {
947:     log(error.stack || error.message);
948:     process.exit(1);
949:   });
950:   return true;
951: }
952: 
953: function installSignalHandlers() {
954:   for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM', 'SIGHUP', 'SIGQUIT']) {
955:     process.on(signal, () => {
956:       if (childProcess && !childProcess.killed) {
957:         childProcess.once('exit', () => {
958:           clearLeaseFile();
959:           process.exit(128);
960:         });
961:         childProcess.kill(signal);
962:         setTimeout(() => {
963:           clearAllLeaseFiles();
964:           process.exit(128);
965:         }, 5000).unref();
966:         return;
967:       }
968:       clearAllLeaseFiles();
969:       process.exit(128);
970:     });
971:   }
972:   process.on('uncaughtException', (err) => {
973:     try {
974:       clearAllLeaseFiles();
975:     } catch {
976:       // Preserve default uncaughtException crash behavior.
977:     }
978:     throw err;
979:   });
980: }
981: 
982: async function launcherMain() {
983:   const started = now();
984:   const actions = [];
985:   let lockHeld = false;
986:   // Orphan daemon pid recorded by a reclaimed prior owner lease. A SIGKILL'd
987:   // launcher leaves its non-detached child reparented to init while still holding
988:   // the SQLite DB; reap it before spawning a successor so we never run two writers
989:   // against the same DB (mirrors the dead-socket respawn reap and the sibling
990:   // launchers' adopt-or-reap guards).
991:   let reclaimedOrphanPid = null;
992: 
993:   try {
994:     installSignalHandlers();
995:     // Lease cleanup runs unconditionally regardless of child termination path.
996:     process.on('exit', clearAllLeaseFiles);
997:     refreshPaths();
998:     ensureLayout(actions);
999:     refreshPaths();
1000: 
1001:     const strictSingleWriter = !isStrictModeDisabled(process.env.MK_CODE_INDEX_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER);
1002:     if (strictSingleWriter) {
1003:       const ownerLeaseResult = acquireOwnerLeaseFile();
1004:       if (ownerLeaseResult.acquired
1005:           && ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed
1006:           && Number.isInteger(ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed.ownerPid)
1007:           && ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed.ownerPid > 0
1008:           && ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed.ownerPid !== process.pid) {
1009:         reclaimedOrphanPid = ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed.ownerPid;
1010:       }
1011:       if (!ownerLeaseResult.acquired) {
1012:         // A live owner already holds the single-writer lease. Bridge this
1013:         // client's stdio to the owner's IPC socket so additional sessions and
1014:         // MCP reconnects share the one daemon (matches mk-spec-memory-launcher).
1015:         // maybeBridgeLeaseHolder falls back to a LEASE_HELD_BY diagnostic line
1016:         // only when the socket is missing/refused or bridging is disabled.
1017:         log(`liveOwnerDetected: ownerPid=${ownerLeaseResult.holder.ownerPid} classification=${ownerLeaseResult.classification}`);
1018:         await bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld({
1019:           ownerPid: ownerLeaseResult.holder.ownerPid,
1020:           startedAt: ownerLeaseResult.holder.startedAtIso,
1021:           socketPath: typeof ownerLeaseResult.holder.socketPath === 'string' ? ownerLeaseResult.holder.socketPath : null,
1022:         }, {
1023:           respawnChildPid: ownerLeaseResult.holder.ownerPid,
1024:         });
1025:         return;
1026:       }
1027: 
1028:       const leaseResult = isLeaseHeld();
1029:       if (leaseResult.held && !leaseResult.staleReclaimable) {
1030:         clearOwnerLeaseFile();
1031:         await bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld(leaseResult);
1032:         return;
1033:       }
1034:       if (leaseResult.staleReclaimable) {
1035:         log(`staleReclaimed: true${leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : ''}`);
1036:       }
1037:     } else {
1038:       log('MK_CODE_INDEX_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER is disabled; skipping lease check');
1039:     }
1040: 
1041:     lockHeld = await acquireBootstrapLock();
1042:     if (lockHeld) {
1043:       // Auto-migrate DB from the former shared standalone location back to skill-local.
1044:       // The former DB is preserved as a backup (copy, not move).
1045:       // This block now runs ONLY for the single bootstrap-lock winner (lockHeld), AFTER the lock
1046:       // is held and BEFORE the DB is opened by the spawned child (launchServer, below). Previously
1047:       // it ran in the main IIFE before any lock, so two concurrent launchers could both pass the
1048:       // !exists(target) guard and both copy — a lagging copier truncate-overwriting the now-LIVE
1049:       // target DB out from under the winner's daemon (SQLite corruption). Gating on lockHeld makes
1050:       // only one launcher migrate; the COPYFILE_EXCL + pre-copy re-check below is the belt-and-
1051:       // suspenders guarantee that NO launcher ever overwrites an existing (possibly live) target DB.
1052:       // Never auto-seed/migrate into an explicit SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR override
1053:       //   (the operator chose that target); migrate only into the resolved default.
1054:       // Never copy a former DB that a live legacy owner could still be
1055:       //   writing — probe BOTH the former PID lease and the former owner lease for liveness first.
1056:       //   The former owner lease (.code-graph-owner.json) is intentionally NOT copied forward.
1057:       const formerSharedDbDir = path.join(opencodeDir, '.spec-kit', 'code-graph', 'database');
1058:       const migrationTarget = resolvedDbDir();
1059:       if (!process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR
1060:           && !exists(path.join(migrationTarget, 'code-graph.sqlite'))
1061:           && exists(path.join(formerSharedDbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite'))
1062:           && !formerLocationOwnerLive(formerSharedDbDir)) {
1063:         fs.mkdirSync(migrationTarget, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
1064:         const dbFiles = [
1065:           'code-graph.sqlite',
1066:           'code-graph.sqlite-shm',
1067:           'code-graph.sqlite-wal',
1068:           '.code-graph-readiness.json',
1069:           '.mk-code-index-launcher.json',
1070:         ];
1071:         // Re-check the sqlite target immediately before copying so a launcher that
1072:         // raced past the outer guard (e.g. a concurrent migrator that just finished) does not
1073:         // clobber the freshly-migrated DB. EEXIST from COPYFILE_EXCL means another launcher
1074:         // already migrated that file -> treat the target as authoritative and stop migrating.
1075:         let migrated = false;
1076:         if (!exists(path.join(migrationTarget, 'code-graph.sqlite'))) {
1077:           try {
1078:             for (const file of dbFiles) {
1079:               const src = path.join(formerSharedDbDir, file);
1080:               const dst = path.join(migrationTarget, file);
1081:               if (exists(src)) {
1082:                 // COPYFILE_EXCL makes the copy fail with EEXIST rather than truncate-
1083:                 // overwrite an existing (possibly live) target file.
1084:                 fs.copyFileSync(src, dst, fs.constants.COPYFILE_EXCL);
1085:               }
1086:             }
1087:             migrated = true;
1088:           } catch (copyError) {
1089:             if (copyError.code === 'EEXIST') {

1091:               // is authoritative. Do NOT overwrite it.

1093:             } else {
1094:               throw copyError;
1095:             }
1096:           }
1097:         }
1098:         if (migrated) {
1099:           process.stderr.write(
1100:             `[mk-code-index-launcher] migrated DB from ${rel(formerSharedDbDir)} to ${rel(migrationTarget)} (former location preserved)\n`
1101:           );
1102:         }
1103:       }
1104: 
1105:       buildIfNeeded(actions);
1106:       log(`ready: ${JSON.stringify({ start: started, end: now(), actions, server: rel(path.join(kitDir, 'mcp_server', 'dist', 'index.js')) })}`);
1107:     }
1108: 
1109:     // Reap the orphan daemon recorded by the reclaimed prior owner lease before
1110:     // spawning a successor. The bootstrap lock above serializes this so only the
1111:     // winning launcher reaps + respawns; a still-live orphan would otherwise keep

1113:     if (Number.isInteger(reclaimedOrphanPid) && reclaimedOrphanPid > 0) {
1114:       const reapResult = await reapOwnerBeforeRespawn(reclaimedOrphanPid);
1115:       if (!reapResult.allowed) {
1116:         log(`reclaimed orphan daemon pid ${reclaimedOrphanPid} could not be reaped (${reapResult.reason}); not spawning a second daemon`);
1117:         process.exit(0);
1118:       }
1119:     }
1120: 
1121:     writeLeaseFile();
1122:     const reprobe = readLeaseFile();
1123:     if (!reprobe || reprobe.pid !== process.pid) {
1124:       const startedAt = reprobe?.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
1125:       process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${reprobe ? reprobe.pid : 'unknown'} startedAt=${startedAt}\n`);

1127:     }
1128:     launchServer();
1129:   } catch (error) {
1130:     log(`failed: ${JSON.stringify({ start: started, end: now(), actions, error: error.message })}`);
1131:     log(error.stack || error.message);
1132:     process.exit(1);
1133:   } finally {

1137:   }
1138: }

1149:   if (nextPaths.kitDir) kitDir = nextPaths.kitDir;

276: function internalHandshake(socket, initializeFrame) {

291:     const decoder = new StringDecoder('utf8');

294:       reject(new Error('timed out waiting for internal initialize'));

297:     const cleanup = () => {
298:       clearTimeout(timer);

315:           initializeResponse = frame;

325:       buffer = '';
326:       residual = '';
327:       cleanup();
328:       resolve({ residual: remainder, protocolVersion: negotiatedVersion, handshakeObserved: true, initializeResponse });

330:     const onError = (error) => {
331:       cleanup();
332:       reject(error);
333:     };
334:     const onClose = () => {
335:       cleanup();
336:       reject(new Error('socket closed during internal initialize'));
337:     };

339:     socket.on('data', onData);

804:   function onInputClose() {

806:     halfCloseSocketIfReady();
807:     finishIfClientEndedAndIdle();
808:   }
809: 
810:   function onOutputError() {
811:     stop();
812:   }
813: 
814:   async function start() {
815:     const ready = await waitForDaemonReady(socketPath, probe, connect, log, { maxAttempts: maxColdStartAttempts });
816:     if (stopped) return;
817:     if (ready.status !== 'alive') {
818:       enqueueOutputFrame(JSON.stringify({
819:         jsonrpc: '2.0',
820:         id: null,
821:         error: {
822:           code: -32001,
823:           message: `backend unavailable: ${ready.reason ?? 'startup-timeout'}`,
824:           data: { retryable: true },
825:         },
826:       }));
827:       requestOutputEnd();
828:       return;
829:     }
830: 
831:     input.on('data', onInputData);
832:     input.once('end', onInputEnd);
833:     input.once('close', onInputClose);
834:     output.once('error', onOutputError);
835: 
836:     await attachFreshSocket();
837:   }
838: 
839:   return { start, stop };
840: }
841: 
842: module.exports = {
843:   createClassifyFrame,
844:   createSessionProxy,
845:   __testing: {
846:     classifyFrame,
847:     createClassifyFrame,
848:     createFrameSplitter,
849:     createPendingRequestsTracker,
850:     parseFrame,
851:     resolveColdStartAttempts,
852:   },
853: };

(End of file - total 853 lines)

1: ---
2: title: Shell Style Guide
3: description: Coding conventions and formatting standards for Bash scripts in the OpenCode development environment.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "opencode shell style guide"
6:   - "bash formatting standards"
7:   - "shell shebang header"
8:   - "bash naming conventions"
9: importance_tier: normal
10: contextType: implementation
11: ---
12: 
13: # Shell Style Guide
14: 
15: Coding conventions and formatting standards for Bash scripts in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Defines consistent styling rules for Bash scripts to ensure reliability, readability, and maintainability across all OpenCode shell scripts.
24: 
25: ### Scope
26: 
27: Applies to all shell files in:
28: - `.opencode/skills/*/scripts/` - Skill automation scripts
29: - `.opencode/agents/scripts/` - Agent provider scripts
30: - `scripts/` - Build and deployment scripts
31: 
32: ### Key Sources
33: 
34: | File | Evidence |
35: |------|----------|
36: | `lib/common.sh` | Color definitions, logging functions, utilities |
37: | `spec/create.sh` | Strict mode, argument parsing, control flow |
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## 2. FILE STRUCTURE
42: 
43: ### Shebang Line
44: 
45: **ALWAYS** use the portable bash shebang:
46: 
47: ```bash
48: #!/usr/bin/env bash
49: ```
50: 
51: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:1`
52: 
53: ### File Header
54: 
55: Use the standard header format for identification:
56: 
57: ```bash
58: #!/usr/bin/env bash
59: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
60: # COMPONENT: [COMPONENT NAME]
61: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62: # Brief description of what this script does.
63: #
64: # Usage: script.sh [options] <arguments>
65: ```
66: 
67: **Alternative** (detailed header for major scripts):
68: 
69: ```bash
70: #!/usr/bin/env bash
71: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: # SPECKIT: CREATE SPEC FOLDER
73: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74: # Creates spec folder with templates based on documentation level.
75: #
76: # TEMPLATE ARCHITECTURE (v2.0 - CORE + ADDENDUM):
77: #   templates/
78: #   ├── level_1/        # Core only (~270 LOC total)
79: #   ├── level_2/        # Core + Verification (~390 LOC)
80: #   └── level_3/        # Core + Verification + Architecture (~540 LOC)
81: ```
82: 
83: **Evidence**: `spec/create.sh:1-20`
84: 
85: ### Strict Mode
86: 
87: **ALWAYS** enable strict mode immediately after the shebang/header:
88: 
89: ```bash
90: set -euo pipefail
91: ```
92: 
93: | Flag | Purpose |
94: |------|---------|
95: | `-e` | Exit on error (non-zero return) |
96: | `-u` | Error on undefined variables |
97: | `-o pipefail` | Pipe fails if any command fails |
98: 
99: **Evidence**: `spec/create.sh:22`
100: 
101: ### Section Organization
102: 
103: Organize code into numbered sections:
104: 
105: ```bash
106: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
107: # 1. COLOR DEFINITIONS
108: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109: 
110: RED='\033[0;31m'
111: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
112: NC='\033[0m'
113: 
114: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115: # 2. UTILITY FUNCTIONS
116: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
117: 
118: log_info() {
119:     printf "${BLUE}INFO:${NC} %s\n" "$1"
120: }
121: ```
122: 
123: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:9-35`
124: 
125: ---
126: 
127: ## 3. NAMING CONVENTIONS
128: 
129: ### Functions
130: 
131: **Use**: `lowercase_with_underscores`
132: 
133: ```bash
134: # Public functions
135: validate_input() {
136:     local input="$1"
137:     # ...
138: }
139: 
140: process_file() {
141:     local file_path="$1"
142:     # ...
143: }
144: ```
145: 
146: ### Private/Internal Functions
147: 
148: **Use**: Single underscore prefix for helpers:
149: 
150: ```bash
151: # Internal helper function
152: _json_escape() {
153:     local str="$1"
154:     str="${str//\\/\\\\}"
155:     str="${str//\"/\\\"}"
156:     printf '%s' "$str"
157: }
158: ```
159: 
160: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:37-45`
161: 
162: ### Variables
163: 
164: **Use**: `lowercase_with_underscores` for local variables:
165: 
166: ```bash
167: local file_path="$1"
168: local output_dir="${2:-./output}"
169: local is_valid=true
170: ```
171: 
172: ### Constants and Global Variables
173: 
174: **Use**: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants and script-level variables:
175: 
176: ```bash
177: readonly MAX_RETRIES=3
178: readonly DEFAULT_TIMEOUT=30
179: 
180: # Script-level state
181: ERRORS=0
182: WARNINGS=0
183: JSON_MODE=false
184: ```
185: 
186: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:28-31`, `spec/create.sh:24-33`
187: 
188: ---
189: 
190: ## 4. COLOR DEFINITIONS
191: 
192: ### Standard Color Setup
193: 
194: Define colors with terminal detection:
195: 
196: ```bash
197: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
198: # COLOR DEFINITIONS
199: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
200: 
201: RED='\033[0;31m'
202: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
203: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
204: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
205: BOLD='\033[1m'
206: NC='\033[0m'  # No Color / Reset
207: 
208: # Disable colors if not terminal (for piping/logging)
209: if [[ ! -t 1 ]]; then
210:     RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' BLUE='' BOLD='' NC=''
211: fi
212: ```
213: 
214: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:13-22`
215: 
216: ### Usage in Output
217: 
218: ```bash
219: # Colored output
220: printf "${GREEN}SUCCESS:${NC} %s\n" "$message"
221: printf "${RED}ERROR:${NC} %s\n" "$error" >&2
222: printf "${YELLOW}WARNING:${NC} %s\n" "$warning"
223: ```
224: 
225: ---
226: 
227: ## 5. LOGGING FUNCTIONS
228: 
229: ### Standard Logging Pattern
230: 
231: ```bash
232: log_pass() {
233:     local rule="$1"
234:     local message="$2"
235:     printf "${GREEN}[PASS]${NC} ${BOLD}%s${NC}: %s\n" "$rule" "$message"
236: }
237: 
238: log_warn() {
239:     local rule="$1"
240:     local message="$2"
241:     printf "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} ${BOLD}%s${NC}: %s\n" "$rule" "$message" >&2
242:     ((WARNINGS++))
243: }
244: 
245: log_error() {
246:     local rule="$1"
247:     local message="$2"
248:     printf "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} ${BOLD}%s${NC}: %s\n" "$rule" "$message" >&2
249:     ((ERRORS++))
250: }
251: 
252: log_info() {
253:     local rule="$1"
254:     local message="$2"
255:     printf "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} ${BOLD}%s${NC}: %s\n" "$rule" "$message"
256: }
257: ```
258: 
259: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:47-88`
260: 
261: ### Detail Logging
262: 
263: For sub-items or additional context:
264: 
265: ```bash
266: log_detail() {
267:     printf "    - %s\n" "$1"
268: }
269: ```
270: 
271: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:90-92`
272: 
273: ### Inline Comments
274: 
275: Follow the universal commenting principles (see `../shared/universal_patterns.md`):
276: 
277: 1. **Quantity limit:** Maximum 3 comments per 10 lines of code
278: 2. **Focus on purposeful semantics:** Explain WHY something is done, not WHAT it does
279: 3. **Focus on WHY, not WHAT:** Explain intent, constraints, reasoning
280: 4. **No commented-out code:** Delete unused code (git preserves history)
281: 
282: **Good - explains reasoning:**
283: 
284: ```bash
285: # Skip if output directory already exists to prevent data loss
286: if [[ -d "$output_dir" ]]; then
287:     log_error "Output directory already exists"
288:     exit 1
289: fi
290: 
291: # Strict mode must catch pipe failures in complex commands
292: set -o pipefail
293: 
294: # Recover pending writes on startup so a crash cannot lose data
295: source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/transaction.sh"
296: ```
297: 
298: **Bad - narrates implementation:**
299: 
300: ```bash
301: # Set the variable to output
302: output_dir="./output"
303: 
304: # Loop through files
305: for file in *.sh; do
306:     echo "$file"
307: done
308: ```
309: 
310: ### Capitalization
311: 
312: All comment text MUST start with a capital letter:
313: 
314: ```bash
315: # Reverse order preserves dependency chain     # correct
316: # reverse order preserves dependency chain     # wrong
317: ```
318: 
319: **Exceptions**: Directives like `shellcheck disable`, inline code references, and technical identifiers.
320: 
321: ### Function Purpose Comments
322: 
323: Brief description above function:
324: 
325: ```bash
326: # Validate JSON file against schema
327: # Returns 0 on success, 1 on validation error
328: validate_json() {
329:     local file="$1"
330:     local schema="$2"
331:     # implementation
332: }
333: ```
334: 
335: ---
336: 
337: ## 6. VARIABLE HANDLING
338: 
339: ### Always Quote Variables
340: 
341: **ALWAYS** double-quote variable expansions:
342: 
343: ```bash
344: # Correct
345: file_path="$1"
346: echo "$file_path"
347: if [[ -f "$file_path" ]]; then
348: 
349: # WRONG - unquoted variables
350: file_path=$1
351: echo $file_path
352: if [[ -f $file_path ]]; then
353: ```
354: 
355: ### Local Variables
356: 
357: **ALWAYS** declare local variables in functions:
358: 
359: ```bash
360: process_file() {
361:     local file_path="$1"
362:     local output_dir="$2"
363:     local result=""
364: 
365:     # Use locals within function
366:     result=$(do_something "$file_path")
367:     echo "$result"
368: }
369: ```
370: 
371: ### Default Values
372: 
373: Use parameter expansion for defaults:
374: 
375: ```bash
376: # Default value if not set
377: output_dir="${2:-./output}"
378: 
379: # Default value if empty or not set
380: timeout="${TIMEOUT:-30}"
381: 
382: # Error if not set
383: required_var="${REQUIRED_VAR:?Error: REQUIRED_VAR must be set}"
384: ```
385: 
386: **Evidence**: `spec/create.sh:27`
387: 
388: ---
389: 
390: ## 7. CONDITIONAL EXPRESSIONS
391: 
392: ### Use [[ ]] for Tests
393: 
394: **ALWAYS** use `[[ ]]` over `[ ]`:
395: 
396: ```bash
397: # Correct - modern bash test
398: if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
399: if [[ "$string" == "value" ]]; then
400: if [[ "$number" -gt 10 ]]; then
401: 
402: # WRONG - POSIX test (less safe)
403: if [ -f "$file" ]; then
404: if [ "$string" = "value" ]; then
405: ```
406: 
407: ### String Comparisons
408: 
409: ```bash
410: # Equality
411: if [[ "$var" == "value" ]]; then
412: 
413: # Pattern matching
414: if [[ "$var" == *.md ]]; then
415: 
416: # Regex matching
417: if [[ "$var" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
418: 
419: # Empty/non-empty
420: if [[ -z "$var" ]]; then    # Empty
421: if [[ -n "$var" ]]; then    # Non-empty
422: ```
423: 
424: ### File Tests
425: 
426: ```bash
427: if [[ -f "$path" ]]; then   # Is regular file
428: if [[ -d "$path" ]]; then   # Is directory
429: if [[ -e "$path" ]]; then   # Exists
430: if [[ -r "$path" ]]; then   # Is readable
431: if [[ -w "$path" ]]; then   # Is writable
432: if [[ -x "$path" ]]; then   # Is executable
433: ```
434: 
435: ---
436: 
437: ## 8. ARGUMENT PARSING
438: 
439: ### Simple Arguments
440: 
441: ```bash
442: #!/usr/bin/env bash
443: set -euo pipefail
444: 
445: # Positional arguments
446: if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
447:     echo "Usage: $0 <input_file>" >&2
448:     exit 1
449: fi
450: 
451: input_file="$1"
452: output_dir="${2:-./output}"
453: ```
454: 
455: ### Flag Parsing Pattern
456: 
457: ```bash
458: # Initialize defaults
459: JSON_MODE=false
460: VERBOSE=false
461: OUTPUT_DIR=""
462: 
463: # Parse arguments
464: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
465:     case "$1" in
466:         --json)
467:             JSON_MODE=true
468:             shift
469:             ;;
470:         --verbose|-v)
471:             VERBOSE=true
472:             shift
473:             ;;
474:         --output|-o)
475:             if [[ $# -lt 2 ]]; then
476:                 echo "Error: --output requires a value" >&2
477:                 exit 1
478:             fi
479:             OUTPUT_DIR="$2"
480:             shift 2
481:             ;;
482:         --help|-h)
483:             show_help
484:             exit 0
485:             ;;
486:         -*)
487:             echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2
488:             exit 1
489:             ;;
490:         *)
491:             # Positional argument
492:             ARGS+=("$1")
493:             shift
494:             ;;
495:     esac
496: done
497: ```
498: 
499: **Evidence**: `spec/create.sh:42-60`
500: 
501: ---
502: 
503: ## 9. FUNCTIONS
504: 
505: ### Function Definition
506: 
507: ```bash
508: # Function with documentation
509: validate_file() {
510:     # Validate that a file exists and is readable
511:     # Args:
512:     #   $1 - File path to validate
513:     # Returns:
514:     #   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
515: 
516:     local file_path="$1"
517: 
518:     if [[ ! -f "$file_path" ]]; then
519:         log_error "FILE" "Not found: $file_path"
520:         return 1
521:     fi
522: 
523:     if [[ ! -r "$file_path" ]]; then
524:         log_error "FILE" "Not readable: $file_path"
525:         return 1
526:     fi
527: 
528:     return 0
529: }
530: ```
531: 
532: ### Return Values
533: 
534: Use return codes for success/failure:
535: 
536: ```bash
537: # Return 0 for success
538: do_something || return 1
539: 
540: # Return specific codes for different errors
541: validate() {
542:     [[ -f "$1" ]] || return 1  # File not found
543:     [[ -r "$1" ]] || return 2  # Not readable
544:     return 0                    # Success
545: }
546: ```
547: 
548: ---
549: 
550: ## 10. ERROR HANDLING
551: 
552: ### Exit on Error
553: 
554: ```bash
555: # Exit immediately on error (from strict mode)
556: set -e
557: 
558: # Or check explicitly
559: command || {
560:     echo "Error: command failed" >&2
561:     exit 1
562: }
563: ```
564: 
565: ### Trap for Cleanup
566: 
567: ```bash
568: cleanup() {
569:     # Remove temp files
570:     [[ -n "${TEMP_FILE:-}" ]] && rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"
571: }
572: 
573: trap cleanup EXIT
574: ```
575: 
576: ### Error Messages to stderr
577: 
578: ```bash
579: # Always send errors to stderr
580: echo "Error: something went wrong" >&2
581: printf "${RED}ERROR:${NC} %s\n" "$message" >&2
582: ```
583: 
584: ---
585: 
586: ## 11. OUTPUT FORMATTING
587: 
588: ### Printf vs Echo
589: 
590: Prefer `printf` for formatted output:
591: 
592: ```bash
593: # Printf - more portable, better formatting
594: printf "Processing: %s\n" "$filename"
595: printf "Found %d files\n" "$count"
596: printf "${GREEN}SUCCESS${NC}\n"
597: 
598: # Echo - simple messages only
599: echo "Starting process..."
600: ```
601: 
602: ### JSON Output
603: 
604: ```bash
605: # JSON output mode
606: if [[ "$JSON_MODE" == true ]]; then
607:     printf '{"status":"success","count":%d}\n' "$count"
608: else
609:     printf "Found %d items\n" "$count"
610: fi
611: ```
612: 
613: ---
614: 
615: ## 12. RELATED RESOURCES
616: 
617: ### Internal References
618: - [quality_standards.md](./quality_standards.md) - Quality requirements
619: - [quick_reference.md](./quick_reference.md) - Quick lookup
620: 
621: ### External Standards
622: - [Google Shell Style Guide](https://google.github.io/styleguide/shellguide.html)
623: - [ShellCheck](https://www.shellcheck.net/) - Static analysis tool

(End of file - total 623 lines)
</content>
